baseline.zh: 营养生长期 · 标准光周期 · 中等处理强度 · 其余条件适宜
baseline.en: Vegetative stage · standard photoperiod · moderate treatment · otherwise favorable conditions

title.zh: 渐进中度干旱下拟南芥叶片的条件性响应轨迹
title.en: Conditional response trajectory of Arabidopsis leaves under progressive moderate drought

summary.zh: 早期水分亏缺首先触发保卫细胞信号和节水反应，明显形态变化尚不预期。
summary.en: Early water deficit first engages guard-cell signaling and water-saving responses, before conspicuous morphology is expected.

text.zh: ABA、Ca²⁺和氧化还原信号增强；保卫细胞离子外流和膨压下降，而叶肉细胞总体仍接近稳态。
text.en: ABA, Ca²⁺, and redox signaling increase; guard-cell ion efflux and turgor decline, while most mesophyll cells remain near steady state.

text.zh: 气孔导度和叶片蒸腾开始下降，叶片含水状态仅轻度受影响。
text.en: Stomatal conductance and leaf transpiration begin to decrease, with only a mild effect on leaf water status.

text.zh: 全株耗水降低；可见萎蔫和生长差异通常尚不明显。
text.en: Whole-plant water use decreases; visible wilting and growth differences are usually not yet pronounced.

text.zh: 早期ABA介导的气孔响应具有较强机制支持，但起始速度取决于实际失水速率。
text.en: Early ABA-mediated stomatal responses are well supported mechanistically, but onset depends on the actual rate of water loss.

summary.zh: 节水信号持续，并开始重塑渗透调节、抗氧化和脱水应答转录。
summary.en: Water-saving signaling persists and begins to reprogram osmotic, antioxidant, and dehydration-responsive transcription.

text.zh: 保卫细胞膨压继续降低；叶肉细胞诱导脱水应答基因、可溶性渗透物和ROS缓冲能力。
text.en: Guard-cell turgor continues to fall; mesophyll cells induce dehydration-response genes, soluble osmolytes, and ROS-buffering capacity.

text.zh: 气孔关闭更一致，胞间CO₂供应和蒸腾进一步下降。
text.en: Stomatal closure becomes more uniform, further decreasing intercellular CO₂ supply and transpiration.

text.zh: 全株水分利用转向保守，叶片扩张速率开始低于对照。
text.en: Whole-plant water use shifts toward conservation, and leaf expansion begins to lag behind the control.

text.zh: 转录重编程的幅度受叶龄、基因型及土壤水势轨迹影响。
text.en: The magnitude of transcriptional reprogramming depends on leaf age, genotype, and the soil-water-potential trajectory.

summary.zh: 适应性渗透调节与光合限制并行出现，叶肉代谢开始承受碳输入不足。
summary.en: Adaptive osmotic adjustment emerges alongside photosynthetic limitation, and mesophyll metabolism begins to experience reduced carbon input.

text.zh: 渗透保护物与抗氧化酶系统上调；因CO₂受限，叶绿体碳同化通量下降并伴随适度光氧化压力。
text.en: Osmoprotectants and antioxidant systems rise; CO₂ limitation lowers chloroplastic carbon-assimilation flux and creates modest photo-oxidative pressure.

text.zh: 净光合速率和叶肉CO₂同化下降，气孔限制仍占主导。
text.en: Net photosynthesis and mesophyll CO₂ assimilation decline, with stomatal limitation still dominant.

text.zh: 生物量积累速率降低，但在中度胁迫下叶片通常仍保持基本功能。
text.en: Biomass accumulation slows, although leaves usually retain basic function under moderate stress.

text.zh: 光合限制由气孔还是非气孔过程主导，取决于实际叶水势和光强。
text.en: Whether stomatal or non-stomatal processes dominate photosynthetic limitation depends on actual leaf water potential and irradiance.

summary.zh: 持续干旱使节水、抗氧化和碳分配调整形成较稳定的叶片应答状态。
summary.en: Sustained drought establishes a more stable leaf response combining water conservation, antioxidant defense, and altered carbon allocation.

text.zh: 细胞维持渗透调节和ROS清除，同时糖代谢与蛋白质周转向维持和保护倾斜。
text.en: Cells maintain osmotic adjustment and ROS scavenging while shifting sugar metabolism and protein turnover toward maintenance and protection.

text.zh: 叶片水势、气孔导度和净光合维持在低于对照的水平。
text.en: Leaf water potential, stomatal conductance, and net photosynthesis remain below control levels.

text.zh: 叶面积增长和全株碳收益受抑，尚可能没有不可逆的叶片损伤。
text.en: Leaf-area growth and whole-plant carbon gain are suppressed, yet irreversible leaf injury may still be absent.

text.zh: 中度处理可能允许部分稳态适应；若失水快于预期，损伤会提前出现。
text.en: Moderate treatment may permit partial steady-state acclimation; injury would occur earlier if dehydration is faster than expected.

summary.zh: 叶片进入维持与防御优先的状态，生长相关过程进一步被资源限制。
summary.en: Leaves enter a maintenance- and defense-prioritized state, with further resource limitation of growth-related processes.

text.zh: 保护性代谢和自噬/蛋白质修复倾向增强；细胞伸长所需的膨压驱动和合成活动受限。
text.en: Protective metabolism and autophagy/protein repair tendencies increase; turgor-driven cell expansion and biosynthesis are constrained.

text.zh: 叶片膨压和细胞扩张下降，叶温可能因蒸腾冷却减弱而升高。
text.en: Leaf turgor and cell expansion decline, while leaf temperature may rise as transpirational cooling weakens.

text.zh: 冠层蒸腾和相对生长速率继续低于对照，轻度叶片下垂可能开始可见。
text.en: Canopy transpiration and relative growth rate remain below control, and mild leaf drooping may begin to be visible.

text.zh: 叶温和可见叶姿变化对湿度、风速及盆栽限根程度敏感。
text.en: Leaf temperature and visible posture changes are sensitive to humidity, airflow, and pot-root restriction.

summary.zh: 若干旱持续，光系统保护需求升高，非气孔性光合限制逐渐变得重要。
summary.en: If drought persists, photoprotective demand rises and non-stomatal limitations to photosynthesis become increasingly important.

text.zh: 叶绿体能量耗散和抗氧化需求增强；部分叶肉细胞的代谢容量下降，但尚可通过保护机制缓冲。
text.en: Chloroplast energy dissipation and antioxidant demand increase; metabolic capacity declines in some mesophyll cells but may still be buffered by protective mechanisms.

text.zh: 光化学效率和羧化能力可能开始下降，叶片水分亏缺更均一。
text.en: Photochemical efficiency and carboxylation capacity may begin to decline, while water deficit becomes more uniform across the leaf.

text.zh: 全株碳同化和新叶生长明显受抑，资源更多投向存活而非扩张。
text.en: Whole-plant carbon assimilation and new-leaf growth are clearly suppressed, with resources directed toward survival rather than expansion.

text.zh: 非气孔限制是否显著取决于胁迫强度是否仍处于真正的中度范围。
text.en: The prominence of non-stomatal limitation depends on whether stress remains genuinely moderate.

summary.zh: 持续胁迫下，叶片在适应与累积损耗之间达到暂时平衡。
summary.en: Under continued stress, leaves reach a temporary balance between acclimation and accumulating metabolic cost.

text.zh: 渗透与氧化还原稳态仍被积极维持，但ATP和还原力更多用于防御、修复及离子稳态。
text.en: Osmotic and redox homeostasis are actively maintained, but more ATP and reductant are used for defense, repair, and ion homeostasis.

text.zh: 气孔导度保持偏低，叶肉同化和叶片水力传导继续受限。
text.en: Stomatal conductance remains low, while mesophyll assimilation and leaf hydraulic conductance remain constrained.

text.zh: 生长抑制持续；若未出现极端失水，老叶可暂时保持绿色。
text.en: Growth suppression persists; if extreme dehydration is avoided, older leaves may temporarily remain green.

text.zh: 维持期长短受土壤补水、根系容量和叶片发育阶段共同决定。
text.en: The duration of this maintenance phase depends on soil water supply, root capacity, and leaf developmental stage.

summary.zh: 长期节水状态使碳饥饿风险和氧化损伤风险增加，但中度胁迫下仍可能保持可逆。
summary.en: Prolonged water conservation increases risks of carbon shortage and oxidative damage, although effects may remain reversible under moderate stress.

text.zh: 储备碳利用增加，防御代谢持续；部分细胞出现更高的膜脂重塑和修复负担。
text.en: Use of carbon reserves increases and defense metabolism persists; some cells carry a greater burden of membrane-lipid remodeling and repair.

text.zh: 叶片净同化、蒸腾及膨压维持能力进一步降低，边缘区域更易先受影响。
text.en: Leaf net assimilation, transpiration, and turgor maintenance decline further, with marginal regions more likely to be affected first.

text.zh: 全株生长停滞趋势增强，叶片可能出现轻度卷曲而非必然黄化。
text.en: Whole-plant growth shows stronger arrest, and leaves may display mild curling rather than necessarily yellowing.

text.zh: 是否出现卷叶或黄化高度依赖叶龄、空气干燥度和实际土壤含水量。
text.en: Whether curling or yellowing appears strongly depends on leaf age, air dryness, and actual soil water content.

summary.zh: 防御性稳态逐渐接近代谢容量上限，叶片功能下降比早期更易被检测到。
summary.en: Defensive homeostasis approaches metabolic capacity limits, making reduced leaf function more readily detectable than at early stages.

text.zh: 抗氧化和渗透调节仍活跃，但持续ROS压力可使部分叶绿体和膜系统效率降低。
text.en: Antioxidant and osmotic programs remain active, but persistent ROS pressure can reduce efficiency of some chloroplast and membrane systems.

text.zh: 叶绿素荧光表现和气体交换预计持续低于对照，局部组织异质性加大。
text.en: Chlorophyll-fluorescence performance and gas exchange are expected to remain below control, with greater local tissue heterogeneity.

text.zh: 叶片扩张基本受限，全株干物质积累进一步落后于对照。
text.en: Leaf expansion is largely constrained, and whole-plant dry-matter accumulation falls further behind the control.

text.zh: 局部组织异质性难以仅由处理描述预测，需结合叶水势和空间成像判定。
text.en: Local tissue heterogeneity cannot be predicted from treatment description alone and requires leaf-water-potential and spatial imaging data.

summary.zh: 若无复水，适应性关闭和代谢损耗共同导致持续的生产力下降。
summary.en: Without rewatering, adaptive closure and metabolic cost together cause sustained loss of productivity.

text.zh: 细胞继续优先维持离子、膜和蛋白稳态；合成代谢与细胞周期活动相对下降。
text.en: Cells continue to prioritize ion, membrane, and protein homeostasis; anabolic metabolism and cell-cycle activity decline relatively.

text.zh: 叶片水力和光合功能处于受限平台，恢复潜力开始取决于是否已有结构性损伤。
text.en: Leaf hydraulic and photosynthetic function reside on a constrained plateau, and recovery potential begins to depend on whether structural injury has occurred.

text.zh: 全株生长显著低于对照，叶片姿态和温度可成为可见的胁迫表型。
text.en: Whole-plant growth is markedly below control, and leaf posture and temperature can become visible stress phenotypes.

text.zh: 结构性损伤的阈值未由方案给出，因此恢复性判断仍不确定。
text.en: The threshold for structural injury is not specified by the protocol, so recoverability remains uncertain.

summary.zh: 叶片可能维持受抑但存活的状态，或在水分进一步下降时转向早期损伤。
summary.en: Leaves may remain in a suppressed but viable state, or shift toward early injury if water availability declines further.

text.zh: 存活细胞维持保护性基因表达和代谢重分配；高亏水微区可能累积膜损伤和ROS。
text.en: Viable cells maintain protective gene expression and metabolic reallocation; highly dehydrated microsites may accumulate membrane damage and ROS.

text.zh: 整体叶片仍低导度、低同化，但局部边缘或老化区域可能先出现功能衰退。
text.en: The overall leaf remains low in conductance and assimilation, but marginal or aging regions may show earlier functional decline.

text.zh: 全株以保存现有叶片为主，新生长很少；轻度卷叶或下垂可能加重。
text.en: The plant prioritizes retaining existing leaves, with little new growth; mild curling or drooping may intensify.

text.zh: 此时结果对实际盆土水分和叶片年龄特别敏感，可能出现不同叶片间分化。
text.en: At this stage, outcomes are especially sensitive to actual pot-soil moisture and leaf age, and leaves may diverge in response.

summary.zh: 在持续中度干旱的条件假设下，叶片预计表现为强节水、低生长和可变程度的可逆功能抑制。
summary.en: Under the assumption of sustained moderate drought, leaves are expected to show strong water conservation, low growth, and variably reversible functional suppression.

text.zh: 适应性渗透和抗氧化程序仍维持，但部分细胞的光合与膜修复能力可能不足以完全抵消累积压力。
text.en: Adaptive osmotic and antioxidant programs persist, but photosynthetic and membrane-repair capacity in some cells may not fully offset accumulated stress.

text.zh: 叶片整体气体交换和光合表现下降；若水势持续恶化，可出现局部黄化或早期坏死。
text.en: Whole-leaf gas exchange and photosynthetic performance decline; if water potential continues to worsen, localized yellowing or early necrosis may appear.

text.zh: 全株碳收益和生长显著受抑；复水后能否恢复取决于是否跨越损伤阈值。
text.en: Whole-plant carbon gain and growth are strongly suppressed; recovery after rewatering depends on whether an injury threshold has been crossed.

text.zh: 处理被定义为中度但未给出叶水势、基因型和叶龄，72小时的损伤程度仍有较大条件依赖性。
text.en: Although treatment is defined as moderate, leaf water potential, genotype, and leaf age are unspecified, making injury severity at 72 h condition-dependent.

mechanisms.zh: 根源和叶片ABA信号促进保卫细胞离子外流与气孔关闭，优先降低蒸腾失水。
mechanisms.en: Root-derived and leaf ABA signaling promotes guard-cell ion efflux and stomatal closure, preferentially reducing transpirational water loss.

mechanisms.zh: 水分亏缺诱导渗透调节、ROS清除、膜脂重塑及蛋白质修复，以维持细胞稳态。
mechanisms.en: Water deficit induces osmotic adjustment, ROS scavenging, membrane-lipid remodeling, and protein repair to maintain cellular homeostasis.

mechanisms.zh: 持续低气孔导度限制CO₂供应；随胁迫延长，叶绿体代谢与光保护限制可叠加。
mechanisms.en: Persistently low stomatal conductance limits CO₂ supply; with prolonged stress, chloroplast metabolic and photoprotective limitations can accumulate.

mechanisms.zh: 碳和能量从叶片扩张转向维持、防御和存活，造成全株生长与干物质积累下降。
mechanisms.en: Carbon and energy are redirected from leaf expansion toward maintenance, defense, and survival, reducing whole-plant growth and dry-matter accumulation.

assumptions.zh: 处理确为渐进、持续的中度干旱，未发生快速致死性脱水或复水脉冲。
assumptions.en: The treatment is genuinely progressive, sustained moderate drought, without rapid lethal dehydration or rewatering pulses.

assumptions.zh: 比较对象为同龄、同一光周期和营养条件下的未处理植株。
assumptions.en: The comparator is an untreated plant of the same age under the same photoperiod and nutritional conditions.

assumptions.zh: 叶片处于营养生长阶段且未受病原、盐害、极端高温或营养缺乏等额外胁迫。
assumptions.en: Leaves are at the vegetative stage and are not exposed to additional pathogen, salinity, extreme-heat, or nutrient-deficiency stress.

assumptions.zh: 基因型、叶龄、土壤水势和空气湿度未明确；这些因素限制后期损伤程度和恢复性的可转移性。
assumptions.en: Genotype, leaf age, soil water potential, and air humidity are unspecified; these factors limit transferability of later injury severity and recoverability.

readouts.zh: 在各预报时点测定盆土含水量、叶水势或相对含水量，以及气孔导度和蒸腾。
readouts.en: At each forecast horizon, measure pot-soil water content, leaf water potential or relative water content, stomatal conductance, and transpiration.

readouts.zh: 测定净光合速率、胞间CO₂、叶温及叶绿素荧光，以区分气孔和非气孔限制。
readouts.en: Measure net photosynthesis, intercellular CO₂, leaf temperature, and chlorophyll fluorescence to distinguish stomatal from non-stomatal limitation.

readouts.zh: 检测ABA、渗透物、ROS/脂质过氧化指标及抗氧化能力，并进行脱水应答转录分析。
readouts.en: Assess ABA, osmolytes, ROS/lipid-peroxidation markers, antioxidant capacity, and dehydration-response transcription.

readouts.zh: 记录叶面积增长、叶片卷曲/下垂、黄化或坏死，并在末期设置复水恢复评估。
readouts.en: Record leaf-area growth, curling/drooping, yellowing or necrosis, and include a terminal rewatering recovery assessment.

design.zh: 采用同龄、随机化的充分浇水对照与渐进干旱组；在6、12、24、36、48、60和72小时进行重复取样，并同步记录实际土壤和叶片水分状态。可加入ABA信号或气孔功能受扰材料作为机制对照，但应将其与野生型背景分别解释。
design.en: Use age-matched, randomized well-watered controls and progressive-drought plants; sample repeatedly at 6, 12, 24, 36, 48, 60, and 72 h while recording actual soil and leaf water status. ABA-signaling or stomatal-function perturbation lines can serve as mechanistic controls, but should be interpreted separately from the wild-type background.

title.zh: 拟南芥叶片中度干旱后复水的恢复轨迹
title.en: Recovery trajectory after rewatering moderately droughted Arabidopsis leaves

summary.zh: 复水首先解除部分水分限制；膨压和气孔状态可迅速响应，但代谢与生长恢复尚不同步。
summary.en: Rewatering first relieves water limitation; turgor and stomatal state can respond rapidly, whereas metabolism and growth remain asynchronous.

text.zh: 叶肉细胞水势与膨压回升，液泡再充水；脱水相关渗透调节仍暂时保留。
text.en: Mesophyll water potential and turgor rise as vacuoles refill; dehydration-associated osmotic adjustment is temporarily retained.

text.zh: 叶片含水状态与叶肉水力连通性改善，萎蔫逐步缓解。
text.en: Leaf hydration and mesophyll hydraulic connectivity improve, progressively relieving wilting.

text.zh: 叶片挺度和白天蒸腾恢复，但气孔导度通常仍低于同龄未处理植株。
text.en: Leaf rigidity and daytime transpiration recover, but stomatal conductance commonly remains below age-matched untreated plants.

text.zh: 中度干旱下早期水分恢复较可信；恢复速度受土壤复湿均一性和蒸汽压亏缺影响。
text.en: Early hydration recovery is well supported for moderate drought; its rate depends on uniform soil rewetting and vapor-pressure deficit.

summary.zh: 水分胁迫信号开始衰减，复水诱导的氧化还原与钙信号参与协调气孔和代谢重启。
summary.en: Water-stress signaling begins to decline, while rewatering-associated redox and calcium signals help coordinate stomatal and metabolic restart.

text.zh: ABA响应与部分保护性转录逐渐下降，而短暂的ROS/Ca²⁺再水化信号可出现。
text.en: ABA responses and some protective transcripts decline, while transient ROS/Ca²⁺ rehydration signals may emerge.

text.zh: 保卫细胞与叶肉细胞间的水分交换更协调，叶脉至叶肉的供水恢复。
text.en: Water exchange between guard and mesophyll cells becomes more coordinated, with vascular-to-mesophyll supply recovering.

text.zh: 气孔可开始部分开放，CO₂供应改善；净同化恢复通常滞后于叶片复水。
text.en: Stomata may begin to partially reopen and improve CO₂ supply; net assimilation usually lags behind leaf rehydration.

text.zh: 信号具有阶段性且受光周期影响，具体峰值时间不确定。
text.en: These signals are phase-dependent and photoperiod-sensitive, so precise peak timing is uncertain.

summary.zh: 在水分供应改善后，气孔限制减弱，光合电子传递和碳同化逐步重建。
summary.en: As water supply improves, stomatal limitation eases and photosynthetic electron transport and carbon assimilation are progressively rebuilt.

text.zh: 叶绿体ATP/NADPH利用与卡尔文循环通量上升；持续的光保护和抗氧化需求仍存在。
text.en: Chloroplast ATP/NADPH use and Calvin-cycle flux increase; photoprotection and antioxidant demand persist.

text.zh: 叶肉光合能力在叶片内恢复，但先前受旱较强区域可能恢复较慢。
text.en: Mesophyll photosynthetic capacity recovers across the leaf, though previously more droughted zones may recover more slowly.

text.zh: 净光合速率和蒸腾增加，叶温逐渐接近未处理对照。
text.en: Net photosynthesis and transpiration increase, and leaf temperature trends toward the untreated control.

text.zh: 恢复取决于干旱期间是否发生持续性光系统或膜损伤。
text.en: Recovery depends on whether drought caused persistent photosystem or membrane damage.

summary.zh: 复水后获得的碳先用于修复、呼吸和渗透物质重调，未必立即转化为叶面积增长。
summary.en: Carbon gained after rewatering is initially allocated to repair, respiration, and osmolyte readjustment rather than immediately to leaf-area growth.

text.zh: 可溶性糖与脯氨酸等兼容溶质开始回调，而修复相关蛋白周转和呼吸底物消耗增强。
text.en: Soluble sugars and compatible solutes such as proline begin readjusting, while repair-associated protein turnover and respiratory substrate use increase.

text.zh: 叶肉可恢复碳输出，但部分碳仍优先投入维持与修复，源叶输出提升有限。
text.en: Mesophyll carbon export can recover, but some carbon remains prioritized for maintenance and repair, limiting the increase in source-leaf export.

text.zh: 光合表现继续改善；可见叶片扩展仅开始恢复，仍落后于未处理植株。
text.en: Photosynthetic performance continues improving; visible leaf expansion only begins to recover and remains behind untreated plants.

text.zh: 碳在修复与生长间的分配受叶龄和干旱持续时间影响。
text.en: Partitioning between repair and growth depends on leaf age and drought duration.

summary.zh: 气孔开放、叶肉羧化和水力供给趋于匹配，但保守的失水控制仍可能限制完全恢复。
summary.en: Stomatal opening, mesophyll carboxylation, and hydraulic supply become better matched, although conservative water-loss control may still limit full recovery.

text.zh: 保卫细胞中的ABA维持效应及离子保留逐步减弱，促进孔径调节恢复。
text.en: ABA maintenance effects and ion retention in guard cells progressively decline, supporting recovery of aperture regulation.

text.zh: 叶片水力导度和气孔斑块化状态改善，叶内气体交换更均一。
text.en: Leaf hydraulic conductance and stomatal patchiness improve, making within-leaf gas exchange more uniform.

text.zh: 气孔导度和同化速率接近对照方向，水分利用策略仍可能偏保守。
text.en: Stomatal conductance and assimilation move toward control values, though water-use behavior may remain conservative.

text.zh: 气孔恢复对当时光照、湿度和根区供水尤为敏感。
text.en: Stomatal recovery is especially sensitive to prevailing light, humidity, and root-zone water supply.

stage.zh: 细胞修复与再生长准备

summary.zh: 受旱期间积累的损伤逐步清除，细胞从防御优先转向支持叶片扩展的状态。
summary.en: Damage accumulated during drought is progressively cleared, shifting cells from defense prioritization toward a state supporting leaf expansion.

text.zh: 膜脂、蛋白质和叶绿体组分修复增强，细胞伸展所需的能量与壁松弛能力上升。
text.en: Repair of membrane lipids, proteins, and chloroplast components increases, along with energy supply and wall-loosening capacity for cell expansion.

text.zh: 叶肉细胞恢复膨大，叶片细胞间隙水分状态趋稳。
text.en: Mesophyll cells resume enlargement, and water status in leaf intercellular spaces stabilizes.

text.zh: 幼叶或仍具扩展能力的叶片开始出现较明显的面积增长恢复。
text.en: Young leaves, or leaves retaining expansion capacity, begin showing more evident recovery of area growth.

text.zh: 已成熟叶片的面积恢复有限；叶片发育阶段是关键限制。
text.en: Area recovery is limited in fully mature leaves; leaf developmental stage is a key constraint.

summary.zh: 昼夜碳代谢、氮同化与抗氧化网络逐步重建到非胁迫样平衡，但仍可能保留胁迫记忆。
summary.en: Diurnal carbon metabolism, nitrogen assimilation, and antioxidant networks progressively return to a non-stressed-like balance, while stress memory may persist.

text.zh: 胁迫防御代谢物继续下降，而初级代谢和氮碳协调增强。
text.en: Stress-defense metabolites continue declining, whereas primary metabolism and carbon–nitrogen coordination strengthen.

text.zh: 叶肉代谢在组织尺度上更均一，维管输出与局部需求的耦合改善。
text.en: Mesophyll metabolism becomes more uniform at tissue scale, with improved coupling of vascular export to local demand.

text.zh: 日间同化和夜间恢复呼吸趋于稳定，叶片功能表现更接近对照。
text.en: Daytime assimilation and nocturnal recovery respiration become more stable, bringing leaf function closer to the control.

text.zh: 部分转录和表观遗传性胁迫记忆可在生理恢复后持续。
text.en: Some transcriptional and epigenetic stress memory may persist after physiological recovery.

summary.zh: 叶片水力、气体交换和同化输出的耦合加强，恢复由快速复水转为功能巩固。
summary.en: Coupling among leaf hydraulics, gas exchange, and assimilate export strengthens, shifting recovery from rapid rehydration to functional consolidation.

text.zh: 细胞水分状态大体稳定，剩余变化主要是代谢和蛋白质组分的精细调整。
text.en: Cellular water status is broadly stable; remaining changes are chiefly fine metabolic and proteomic adjustments.

text.zh: 叶脉供水、叶肉同化与韧皮部装载的协调性提高。
text.en: Coordination among vascular water supply, mesophyll assimilation, and phloem loading improves.

text.zh: 叶片日变化的气孔和光合响应更接近未处理植株，但最大表现可能仍略低。
text.en: Diurnal stomatal and photosynthetic responses more closely resemble untreated plants, though peak performance may remain slightly lower.

text.zh: 是否达到对照水平取决于受旱损伤的可逆性与复水后的环境稳定性。
text.en: Whether control-level function is reached depends on drought-damage reversibility and environmental stability after rewatering.

summary.zh: 若叶片仍处于可扩展阶段，恢复的碳和水分供应可支持部分补偿生长；成熟叶则主要恢复功能而非面积。
summary.en: If leaves remain expandable, recovered carbon and water supply can support partial compensatory growth; mature leaves chiefly recover function rather than area.

text.zh: 细胞壁松弛、糖利用和细胞扩展活动增强，但受既往生长停滞限制。
text.en: Cell-wall loosening, sugar use, and cell expansion increase, but are constrained by the preceding growth arrest.

text.zh: 扩展区细胞增大促进叶片厚度和面积的有限恢复。
text.en: Cell enlargement in expansion zones promotes limited recovery of leaf thickness and area.

text.zh: 叶面积增长速率可高于受旱期，但不应假定能够完全补偿既往损失。
text.en: Leaf-area growth may exceed its drought-period rate, but complete compensation for prior loss should not be assumed.

text.zh: 补偿生长高度依赖叶龄；方案未明确叶片发育位置。
text.en: Compensatory growth is strongly leaf-age dependent; the developmental position of the sampled leaf is unspecified.

summary.zh: 多数快速生理指标进入平台，后续差异主要反映残留损伤、叶龄和资源再分配。
summary.en: Most rapid physiological indicators enter a plateau; later differences mainly reflect residual damage, leaf age, and resource reallocation.

text.zh: 渗透状态和核心能量代谢接近稳定，防御相关残余调整缓慢消退。
text.en: Osmotic status and core energy metabolism approach stability, while residual defense-related adjustments fade slowly.

text.zh: 叶片水力与光合组织功能基本稳定，局部受损区域可能保持异质性。
text.en: Leaf hydraulic and photosynthetic tissue function is largely stable, although locally damaged areas may retain heterogeneity.

text.zh: 净同化、蒸腾和叶温接近稳定恢复水平，未必与对照完全重合。
text.en: Net assimilation, transpiration, and leaf temperature approach stable recovered levels, which may not fully coincide with controls.

text.zh: 平台高度取决于干旱遗留效应，不能仅凭复水时间推断。
text.en: Plateau height depends on drought legacy effects and cannot be inferred from rewatering duration alone.

summary.zh: 复水后的残留防御状态继续重整；资源可由修复逐渐转向正常叶片维持和输出。
summary.en: Residual defense states after rewatering continue to resolve; resources can progressively shift from repair toward normal leaf maintenance and export.

text.zh: 抗氧化和脱水保护程序的基础激活度下降，代谢物库向生长和输出需求重配。
text.en: Basal activation of antioxidant and dehydration-protection programs declines, and metabolite pools are repartitioned toward growth and export demands.

text.zh: 源叶向维管系统的同化物输出进一步恢复，组织间资源竞争减弱。
text.en: Assimilate export from the source leaf to the vasculature recovers further, reducing intertissue resource competition.

text.zh: 整片叶的碳获取和输出能力可继续小幅改善。
text.en: Whole-leaf carbon acquisition and export capacity may continue modest improvement.

text.zh: 残留防御的持续时间受基因型和先前胁迫历程影响。
text.en: The persistence of residual defense depends on genotype and prior stress history.

summary.zh: 至此叶片预计实现显著功能恢复，但叶面积、资源储备或胁迫记忆可能仍与同龄未处理植株不同。
summary.en: By this point, leaves are expected to achieve substantial functional recovery, but leaf area, reserves, or stress memory may still differ from age-matched untreated plants.

text.zh: 核心水分与光合代谢接近对照，部分胁迫记忆相关转录或代谢状态仍可保留。
text.en: Core water status and photosynthetic metabolism approach the control, while some stress-memory-associated transcriptional or metabolic states may remain.

text.zh: 叶片组织功能总体稳定；不可逆受损细胞或发育延迟造成的差异不会因复水自动消失。
text.en: Leaf tissue function is broadly stable; differences caused by irreversibly damaged cells or developmental delay do not automatically disappear with rewatering.

text.zh: 气体交换和叶片水分状态接近对照，而叶面积与累计生物量通常仍可能偏低。
text.en: Gas exchange and leaf water status approach the control, whereas leaf area and cumulative biomass may still remain lower.

text.zh: 未给出基因型、叶龄和干旱时长，故完全恢复与遗留表型的程度不确定。
text.en: Genotype, leaf age, and drought duration are unspecified, so the extent of full recovery and legacy phenotypes is uncertain.

mechanisms.zh: 复水先恢复根—叶水力供给和叶肉膨压，随后才逐步解除气孔与生化光合限制。
mechanisms.en: Rewatering first restores root-to-leaf hydraulic supply and mesophyll turgor, then progressively relieves stomatal and biochemical photosynthetic limitations.

mechanisms.zh: ABA衰减与再水化相关ROS/Ca²⁺、氧化还原信号共同重设保卫细胞和叶肉细胞状态。
mechanisms.en: Declining ABA together with rehydration-associated ROS/Ca²⁺ and redox signals resets guard-cell and mesophyll-cell states.

mechanisms.zh: 恢复碳首先服务于修复、呼吸和渗透物质重调，因此功能恢复可快于叶面积和累计生物量恢复。
mechanisms.en: Recovered carbon initially supports repair, respiration, and osmolyte readjustment, so functional recovery can outpace recovery of leaf area and cumulative biomass.

mechanisms.zh: 干旱遗留的膜、光系统或发育损伤，以及胁迫记忆，可使复水轨迹并非干旱过程的简单逆转。
mechanisms.en: Drought legacies in membranes, photosystems, or development, together with stress memory, can make rewatering recovery unlike a simple reversal of drought.

assumptions.zh: 假定为营养充足的营养生长期拟南芥，经历中度、可逆性为主的土壤干旱后一次充分且均一的复水。
assumptions.en: Assumes adequately nourished vegetative Arabidopsis undergoes a moderate, predominantly reversible soil drought followed by one full and uniform rewatering.

assumptions.zh: 方向均相对于每个时间点同龄、未受旱且持续正常供水的植株。
assumptions.en: Directions are relative to age-matched plants that were never droughted and remained normally watered at each timepoint.

assumptions.zh: 假定复水后光照、温度、湿度和根区氧气处于正常范围，且不存在复水积水或额外胁迫。
assumptions.en: Assumes post-rewatering light, temperature, humidity, and root-zone oxygen remain normal, with no waterlogging or additional stress.

assumptions.zh: 未指定基因型、单叶叶龄、干旱持续时间及复水量；这些缺口限制对生长补偿和完全恢复的判断。
assumptions.en: Genotype, individual-leaf age, drought duration, and rewatering volume are unspecified; these gaps limit inference about compensation and full recovery.

readouts.zh: 叶片相对含水量、叶水势、膨压或叶片厚度变化，以解析早期复水和水力恢复。
readouts.en: Leaf relative water content, water potential, turgor, or leaf-thickness dynamics to resolve early rehydration and hydraulic recovery.

readouts.zh: 气孔导度、蒸腾、叶温与叶绿素荧光，并同步测定净光合和胞间CO₂。
readouts.en: Stomatal conductance, transpiration, leaf temperature, and chlorophyll fluorescence, alongside net photosynthesis and intercellular CO₂.

readouts.zh: ABA、脯氨酸、可溶性糖、氧化还原指标及关键胁迫/光合转录本的时间序列。
readouts.en: Time series of ABA, proline, soluble sugars, redox indicators, and key stress/photosynthesis transcripts.

readouts.zh: 叶面积扩展、比叶面积、干重及叶片同化物输出或糖/淀粉周转，以区分功能恢复与生长补偿。
readouts.en: Leaf-area expansion, specific leaf area, dry mass, and assimilate export or sugar/starch turnover to distinguish functional recovery from growth compensation.

design.zh: 设置持续正常供水对照、持续干旱对照和复水组；在复水前及各预测窗口附近进行重复的昼夜一致采样，并记录土壤含水量、VPD和叶龄。将成熟叶与扩展叶分开分析，以检验功能恢复是否快于生长恢复。
design.en: Include continuously watered, continuously droughted, and rewatered groups; sample consistently across the diel cycle before rewatering and near each forecast window, while recording soil water content, VPD, and leaf age. Analyze mature and expanding leaves separately to test whether functional recovery outpaces growth recovery.

title.zh: 拟南芥叶片对非冻害冷胁迫的72小时条件轨迹
title.en: Conditional 72-hour trajectory of Arabidopsis leaves under non-freezing cold stress

summary.zh: 早期冷感知与保护性信号启动，尚未预期出现明显形态改变。
summary.en: Early cold sensing and protective signaling begin, without an expected overt morphological change.

text.zh: 胞质Ca²⁺和活性氧信号短暂增强，而细胞伸长与代谢通量开始受抑。
text.en: Cytosolic Ca²⁺ and reactive-oxygen signals rise transiently, while cell expansion and metabolic flux begin to slow.

text.zh: 叶片气孔导度和瞬时碳同化倾向下降，组织含水状态大体维持。
text.en: Leaf stomatal conductance and instantaneous carbon assimilation tend to decline, while tissue water status is broadly maintained.

text.zh: 全株外观与叶面积通常近似稳定，但生长速率已可能降低。
text.en: Whole-plant appearance and leaf area are usually near stable, although growth rate may already be lower.

text.zh: 早期信号的机制支持较强；实际幅度取决于温度落差与昼夜时相。
text.en: Support for early signaling is strong; magnitude depends on temperature drop and diurnal timing.

summary.zh: 冷响应转录和渗透保护代谢逐步建立，光合限制更易检测。
summary.en: Cold-responsive transcription and osmoprotective metabolism are being established, with more detectable photosynthetic limitation.

text.zh: 冷响应转录本、可溶性糖与抗氧化防御倾向积累，以维持膜与氧化还原稳态。
text.en: Cold-responsive transcripts, soluble sugars, and antioxidant defenses tend to accumulate to support membrane and redox homeostasis.

text.zh: 叶肉光化学效率和CO₂同化能力下降，气孔调节可加重扩散限制。
text.en: Mesophyll photochemical efficiency and CO₂ assimilation decline, with stomatal regulation potentially increasing diffusional limitation.

text.zh: 叶片扩张和全株相对生长率预期降低，但可见伤害通常很轻。
text.en: Leaf expansion and whole-plant relative growth rate are expected to decrease, though visible injury is usually slight.

text.zh: 转录与光合响应的先后受光周期和实际叶温影响。
text.en: The ordering of transcriptional and photosynthetic responses depends on photoperiod and actual leaf temperature.

summary.zh: 保护性重编程扩大，生长相关碳分配开始转向维持与防御。
summary.en: Protective reprogramming expands, and carbon allocation begins shifting from growth toward maintenance and defense.

text.zh: 膜脂重塑、蛋白稳态与兼容溶质相关过程增强，降低冷致结构失稳风险。
text.en: Processes supporting membrane-lipid remodeling, proteostasis, and compatible solutes increase, reducing risk of cold-induced structural instability.

text.zh: 叶片仍受光合受限，但糖积累与抗氧化能力提升可部分缓冲氧化压力。
text.en: Leaves remain photosynthetically constrained, but rising sugar pools and antioxidant capacity can partly buffer oxidative pressure.

text.zh: 新生叶和叶片扩张受抑更明确，植株生物量积累趋缓。
text.en: Inhibition of newly formed leaves and leaf expansion becomes clearer, and plant biomass accumulation slows.

text.zh: 糖积累是否明显取决于光照下的同化残余与夜间消耗。
text.en: Whether sugar accumulation is prominent depends on residual assimilation in light and nighttime consumption.

summary.zh: 经历一个昼夜周期后，冷适应状态趋于可测的稳态，但生长代价显现。
summary.en: After one diurnal cycle, a measurable cold-acclimatory state tends to emerge, with a visible cost to growth.

text.zh: 冷调控基因表达、脱水保护蛋白和渗透调节物的维持水平上升。
text.en: Maintenance levels of cold-regulated expression, dehydration-protective proteins, and osmotic-adjustment metabolites rise.

text.zh: 叶片净光合、电子传递容量及昼间膨压驱动的扩张总体下降。
text.en: Leaf net photosynthesis, electron-transport capacity, and daytime turgor-driven expansion are overall reduced.

text.zh: 叶面积增长和株高增长下降；中等非冻害处理下通常不应出现广泛坏死。
text.en: Leaf-area and height growth decline; widespread necrosis is not generally expected under moderate non-freezing treatment.

text.zh: 该阶段最适于比较处理与同龄未处理对照的累积生长差异。
text.en: This stage is well suited to compare accumulated growth differences against age-matched untreated controls.

summary.zh: 适应与代谢代价并存，叶片在较低生理通量下维持功能。
summary.en: Acclimation and metabolic cost coexist as leaves maintain function at lower physiological flux.

text.zh: 保护性代谢与修复增强，但蛋白合成、细胞周期和细胞壁松弛等生长过程受限。
text.en: Protective metabolism and repair increase, whereas protein synthesis, cell cycling, and cell-wall loosening associated with growth are constrained.

text.zh: 叶肉碳固定和叶片蒸腾维持在较低水平，叶温与能量平衡可能改变。
text.en: Mesophyll carbon fixation and leaf transpiration remain lower, potentially altering leaf temperature and energy balance.

text.zh: 营养生长持续放缓，已展开叶的尺寸变化小于未处理同龄植株。
text.en: Vegetative growth remains slow, and expansion of existing leaves is less than in age-matched untreated plants.

text.zh: 气孔反应方向和幅度受湿度、光强及根系供水状态调节。
text.en: The direction and magnitude of stomatal responses are modulated by humidity, irradiance, and root water supply.

summary.zh: 冷适应网络进入维持期，反复的光照—黑暗转换可暴露能量限制。
summary.en: The cold-acclimation network enters a maintenance phase, and repeated light-dark transitions can expose energy limitation.

text.zh: 抗氧化酶活性、糖代谢调节和膜稳定化能力预计维持较高水平。
text.en: Antioxidant enzyme activity, sugar-metabolism regulation, and membrane-stabilizing capacity are expected to remain elevated.

text.zh: 组织抗氧化缓冲增强，但光系统修复与碳同化仍可能落后于未处理叶片。
text.en: Tissue antioxidant buffering is enhanced, but photosystem repair and carbon assimilation may still lag behind untreated leaves.

text.zh: 整体生物量积累继续低于对照；叶色多保持正常或仅轻度加深。
text.en: Overall biomass accumulation remains below control; leaf color is often normal or only slightly darker.

text.zh: 若夜温更低或光照较强，光抑制风险会提高。
text.en: Photoinhibition risk rises if nights are colder or irradiance is stronger.

summary.zh: 叶片已形成部分耐冷表型，代谢重分配限制后续叶片生产力。
summary.en: Leaves have developed a partial cold-tolerant state, while metabolic reallocation limits subsequent productivity.

text.zh: 渗透调节物、脂质不饱和化相关调节和蛋白质质量控制预计持续增强。
text.en: Osmotic-adjustment metabolites, regulation associated with lipid unsaturation, and protein quality control are expected to remain enhanced.

text.zh: 在稳定冷温下，叶片离子与含水稳态可趋于稳定，但同化水平仍低。
text.en: Under stable cold, leaf ionic and water homeostasis can approach stability, although assimilation remains low.

text.zh: 新叶发生和叶片展开相对对照延迟，株型更紧凑。
text.en: Initiation and unfolding of new leaves are delayed relative to control, producing a more compact habit.

text.zh: 发育延迟程度对处理温度、植株大小和生态型敏感。
text.en: The extent of developmental delay is sensitive to treatment temperature, plant size, and ecotype.

summary.zh: 第二个昼夜周期强化适应性稳态，也累积了持续低温的生长惩罚。
summary.en: A second diurnal cycle reinforces acclimatory homeostasis while accumulating the growth penalty of sustained cold.

text.zh: 主要冷保护程序可保持高于基线的稳定水平，急性信号脉冲则减弱。
text.en: Core cold-protective programs can remain stably above baseline, while acute signaling pulses subside.

text.zh: 单位叶面积气体交换和光合电子传递仍偏低，但组织完整性通常保存。
text.en: Gas exchange and photosynthetic electron transport per leaf area remain low, but tissue integrity is usually preserved.

text.zh: 与同龄对照相比，累计叶面积和干物质增量差距扩大。
text.en: The cumulative gap in leaf area and dry-matter gain versus age-matched controls widens.

text.zh: 中等、非冻害条件下适应而非不可逆损伤是优先假设。
text.en: Under moderate, non-freezing conditions, acclimation rather than irreversible injury is the leading hypothesis.

summary.zh: 长期维持阶段开始，碳收支与夜间储备动员成为关键约束。
summary.en: The longer-term maintenance phase begins, with carbon balance and nighttime reserve mobilization becoming key constraints.

text.zh: 白天保护性糖供应可增加，而夜间呼吸与修复消耗可能压低可用于生长的碳。
text.en: Protective sugar supply may increase by day, whereas nighttime respiration and repair can reduce carbon available for growth.

text.zh: 叶片日尺度净碳增益预期低于对照，尤其在低温黑暗期后更明显。
text.en: Daily net carbon gain of leaves is expected to remain below control, especially after the cold dark period.

text.zh: 植株相对生长率持续降低，幼叶尺寸和展开速度受限。
text.en: Plant relative growth rate remains lower, with restricted young-leaf size and unfolding rate.

text.zh: 碳状态受日长、光合有效辐射和处理开始时刻显著影响。
text.en: Carbon status is strongly influenced by day length, photosynthetically active radiation, and treatment start time.

summary.zh: 若冷温稳定且营养充足，叶片以低通量、较高保护水平持续运行。
summary.en: If cold is stable and nutrition is adequate, leaves continue operating at low flux with elevated protection.

text.zh: 细胞膜、氧化还原和蛋白稳态调节保持重设状态，新增急性损伤信号有限。
text.en: Cellular membrane, redox, and proteostasis regulation remain reset, with limited new acute-damage signaling.

text.zh: 组织完整性和叶片含水量预计稳定；低光合性能不必意味着进行性坏死。
text.en: Tissue integrity and leaf water content are expected to be stable; low photosynthetic performance need not imply progressive necrosis.

text.zh: 全株仍表现为较慢的营养生长和较低的生物量积累。
text.en: The whole plant still shows slower vegetative growth and lower biomass accumulation.

text.zh: 稳定性假设依赖温度无波动、无病害及养分供应充足。
text.en: This stability assumption depends on unfluctuating temperature, absence of disease, and adequate nutrient supply.

summary.zh: 适应性状态持续，但累积生长缺口已成为最清晰的整株输出。
summary.en: The acclimated state persists, while accumulated growth deficit becomes the clearest whole-plant output.

text.zh: 防御和维持资源持续占用碳氮资源，细胞增殖与扩张的资源供给相对受限。
text.en: Defense and maintenance continue consuming carbon and nitrogen resources, relatively limiting supply for cell division and expansion.

text.zh: 叶片光合能力和源叶输出仍低于对照，影响向生长区的碳供应。
text.en: Leaf photosynthetic capacity and source-leaf export remain below control, affecting carbon supply to growing regions.

text.zh: 莲座叶扩展与植株干物质累积的差异预计进一步可辨。
text.en: Differences in rosette expansion and plant dry-matter accumulation are expected to become more discernible.

text.zh: 源—库响应随植株发育阶段变化，营养生长期的推断最适用。
text.en: Source-sink responses vary with developmental stage; this inference is most applicable during vegetative growth.

summary.zh: 72小时内的主要结果应是可逆性冷适应伴随持续的生长抑制，而非冻害。
summary.en: By 72 h, the main expected outcome is reversible cold acclimation with sustained growth inhibition rather than freezing injury.

text.zh: 冷保护、膜稳态和抗氧化调节维持在重设水平，前提是胁迫未加剧。
text.en: Cold protection, membrane homeostasis, and antioxidant regulation remain at reset levels, provided stress has not intensified.

text.zh: 叶片结构和水分状态多可维持，但碳同化与生长性膨压过程仍低于对照。
text.en: Leaf structure and water status can largely be maintained, but carbon assimilation and growth-related turgor processes remain below control.

text.zh: 叶面积、株高及生物量的累计增长低于同龄未处理植株；复温后可能部分恢复。
text.en: Cumulative leaf-area, height, and biomass growth are lower than in age-matched untreated plants; partial recovery may occur after rewarming.

text.zh: 该终点假设中等冷强度且无冻结、长期弱光或继发胁迫。
text.en: This endpoint assumes moderate cold without freezing, prolonged low light, or secondary stress.

mechanisms.zh: 低温感知可通过Ca²⁺、活性氧和激素相关信号触发冷响应转录与代谢重编程。
mechanisms.en: Cold sensing can engage Ca²⁺, reactive-oxygen, and hormone-related signals to trigger cold-responsive transcription and metabolic reprogramming.

mechanisms.zh: 膜脂重塑、渗透调节物积累、抗氧化防御和蛋白稳态有助于维持细胞完整性。
mechanisms.en: Membrane-lipid remodeling, osmotic-adjustment metabolite accumulation, antioxidant defense, and proteostasis can help preserve cellular integrity.

mechanisms.zh: 低温降低酶促反应、气孔—叶肉CO₂供应和光合电子利用效率，使碳分配从生长转向维持与保护。
mechanisms.en: Cold reduces enzymatic rates, stomatal-mesophyll CO₂ supply, and photosynthetic electron use, shifting carbon allocation from growth toward maintenance and protection.

assumptions.zh: 处理为稳定、非冻害且中等强度的低温；未给出确切温度，因此不推断具体反应幅度。
assumptions.en: Treatment is stable, moderate, and non-freezing; because an exact temperature is not provided, no specific response magnitude is inferred.

assumptions.zh: 植株处于营养生长期、叶片健康且养分和水分充足；预测相对同龄未处理植株。
assumptions.en: Plants are vegetative, leaves are healthy, and nutrient and water supply are adequate; predictions are relative to age-matched untreated plants.

assumptions.zh: 稳定光照减少光环境混杂，但光周期时相、湿度、生态型和叶龄仍会改变轨迹。
assumptions.en: Stable light reduces light-environment confounding, but photoperiod phase, humidity, ecotype, and leaf age can still alter the trajectory.

readouts.zh: 在6、12、24、48和72小时测定叶片温度、气孔导度、蒸腾、CO₂同化和叶绿素荧光。
readouts.en: At 6, 12, 24, 48, and 72 h, measure leaf temperature, stomatal conductance, transpiration, CO₂ assimilation, and chlorophyll fluorescence.

readouts.zh: 在6、18、24和48小时检测Ca²⁺/氧化还原信号、冷响应转录标记、可溶性糖、膜脂组成及抗氧化状态。
readouts.en: At 6, 18, 24, and 48 h, assay Ca²⁺/redox signals, cold-response transcript markers, soluble sugars, membrane-lipid composition, and antioxidant status.

readouts.zh: 在24、48和72小时量化叶面积、叶片展开、新叶发生、鲜重/干重及可见伤害评分。
readouts.en: At 24, 48, and 72 h, quantify leaf area, leaf unfolding, new-leaf emergence, fresh/dry mass, and visible-injury scores.

design.zh: 采用同批次、同龄植株的温度匹配对照；随机化位置并记录实际叶温、湿度和昼夜时相。设置复温恢复组，以区分可逆适应与持续损伤。
design.en: Use age-matched plants from the same batch as temperature-matched controls; randomize position and record actual leaf temperature, humidity, and diurnal phase. Include a rewarming-recovery group to distinguish reversible acclimation from persistent damage.

title.zh: 中度干旱下叶片 FRO6 上调的拟南芥条件轨迹
title.en: Conditional trajectory of leaf FRO6 upregulation in Arabidopsis under moderate drought

summary.zh: 早期铁氧化还原与干旱信号首先在细胞层面耦合，尚未预期出现可见叶片表型。
summary.en: Early coupling of iron-redox and drought signaling is expected first at the cellular scale, without a visible leaf phenotype.

text.zh: FRO6 相关的铁还原潜力上升；干旱诱导的 ROS、Ca²⁺与 ABA 信号同步增强。
text.en: FRO6-associated ferric-reduction capacity rises, while drought-induced ROS, Ca²⁺, and ABA signaling also increase.

text.zh: 叶肉整体铁状态和光合组织结构预计基本不变。
text.en: Bulk mesophyll iron status and photosynthetic tissue structure are expected to remain largely unchanged.

text.zh: 整株蒸腾和生长尚主要反映中度干旱本身。
text.en: Whole-plant transpiration and growth still primarily reflect moderate drought itself.

text.zh: 叶片情境提高可迁移性，但 FRO6 的亚细胞作用位点仍限制推断。
text.en: The leaf context improves transferability, but uncertainty about FRO6 subcellular action limits inference.

summary.zh: 持续的 FRO6 活性可能改变可利用铁池，并开始重塑抗氧化与铁稳态转录响应。
summary.en: Sustained FRO6 activity may alter the labile iron pool and begin to reshape antioxidant and iron-homeostasis transcriptional responses.

text.zh: 可还原铁利用度可能升高，同时铁驱动 ROS 生成与抗氧化清除需求均上升。
text.en: Reducible iron availability may increase, while both iron-driven ROS formation and antioxidant-scavenging demand rise.

text.zh: 叶片尚无明确净铁积累或失绿预期，但叶肉细胞间反应可开始分化。
text.en: No clear net leaf iron accumulation or chlorosis is expected yet, although mesophyll-cell responses may begin to diverge.

text.zh: 气孔限制下的整株碳同化尚未出现可归因于 FRO6 的净变化。
text.en: Under stomatal limitation, whole-plant carbon assimilation has not yet developed a net change attributable to FRO6.

text.zh: 方向取决于上调强度及其是否真正提高叶肉可利用铁。
text.en: The direction depends on upregulation strength and on whether it truly increases mesophyll-accessible iron.

summary.zh: 铁稳态和氧化还原缓冲的转录/蛋白层调整应逐渐显现，决定后续是适应还是氧化负担。
summary.en: Transcriptional and protein-level adjustment of iron homeostasis and redox buffering should become apparent, determining whether the later outcome is adaptation or oxidative burden.

text.zh: 铁稳态、ROS 解毒和光合保护相关基因可能被诱导；若游离铁过多，脂质氧化压力也会增加。
text.en: Iron-homeostasis, ROS-detoxification, and photosynthetic-protection programs may be induced; excessive labile iron could also increase lipid-oxidation pressure.

text.zh: 叶肉中铁供给较弱区域可能改善，而高铁或强光微区可能出现氧化压力。
text.en: Iron supply may improve in weakly supplied mesophyll regions, whereas high-iron or high-light microsites may experience oxidative pressure.

text.zh: 叶面积和生物量不会在此早期发生明显改变。
text.en: Leaf area and biomass are not expected to change detectably at this early stage.

text.zh: 缺少表达幅度和叶肉定位信息，净 ROS 结局尚不确定。
text.en: Without expression magnitude and mesophyll-localization information, the net ROS outcome remains uncertain.

summary.zh: 若 FRO6 增强了叶片铁可用性，光合电子传递和抗氧化酶金属化可能获得有限支持。
summary.en: If FRO6 enhances leaf iron availability, photosynthetic electron transport and metallation of antioxidant enzymes may receive limited support.

text.zh: 铁依赖电子传递组分的维持能力和抗氧化缓冲潜力可能提高。
text.en: Capacity to maintain iron-dependent electron-transfer components and antioxidant buffering may increase.

text.zh: 叶肉光化学效率在干旱背景下可能较对照更易维持。
text.en: Mesophyll photochemical efficiency may be more readily maintained than in drought controls.

text.zh: 单位叶面积碳同化可能受保护，但气孔关闭仍限制整株同化。
text.en: Carbon assimilation per leaf area may be protected, but stomatal closure still constrains whole-plant assimilation.

text.zh: 该获益要求铁还原产物可被适当转运和利用。
text.en: This benefit requires reduced iron to be appropriately transported and utilized.

summary.zh: 组织尺度的铁再分配与叶片水分胁迫适应开始整合，保护效应与铁诱导氧化风险并存。
summary.en: Tissue-scale iron redistribution and leaf water-stress adaptation begin to integrate, with protective effects coexisting with iron-induced oxidative risk.

text.zh: 铁利用和 ROS 清除可能持续增强；若缓冲不足，游离铁介导的氧化损伤会累积。
text.en: Iron utilization and ROS scavenging may remain elevated; if buffering is insufficient, labile-iron-mediated oxidative damage can accumulate.

text.zh: 叶绿素与 PSII 功能倾向被维持，但局部失绿或膜损伤风险增加。
text.en: Chlorophyll and PSII function tend to be maintained, but risks of local chlorosis or membrane damage increase.

text.zh: 相对于干旱对照，叶片功能保持可能轻度改善。
text.en: Relative to drought controls, preservation of leaf function may improve modestly.

text.zh: 净效应受初始铁营养、光强和抗氧化能力强烈调节。
text.en: The net effect is strongly conditioned by initial iron nutrition, light intensity, and antioxidant capacity.

summary.zh: 在适度上调的假设下，FRO6 可能支持受旱叶片的光合装置维护，但其收益预计有限。
summary.en: Under the assumption of moderate upregulation, FRO6 may support maintenance of the droughted leaf photosynthetic apparatus, but the benefit is expected to be limited.

text.zh: 铁依赖代谢和抗氧化酶活性维持能力可能高于未上调对照。
text.en: Maintenance capacity for iron-dependent metabolism and antioxidant enzyme activity may exceed that of non-upregulated controls.

text.zh: 叶肉光合细胞的电子传递稳定性和叶绿素保持度可能提高。
text.en: Electron-transport stability and chlorophyll retention in photosynthetic mesophyll cells may improve.

text.zh: 叶片相对含水量与短期光合同化的下降幅度可能减小。
text.en: Declines in leaf relative water content and short-term photosynthetic assimilation may be smaller.

text.zh: 该轨迹是假设温和上调，而非造成铁过载的强制表达。
text.en: This trajectory assumes moderate upregulation rather than forced expression causing iron overload.

summary.zh: 随着干旱延续，FRO6 的潜在保护作用进入平台期，铁负荷相关代价更值得关注。
summary.en: As drought persists, the potential protection from FRO6 approaches a plateau and costs associated with iron loading become more relevant.

text.zh: 抗氧化系统可能仍受支持，但持续的还原铁增加可放大 Fenton 型氧化风险。
text.en: Antioxidant systems may remain supported, but sustained reduced iron can amplify Fenton-type oxidative risk.

text.zh: 多数叶肉区域或保持较好功能，局部区域则可能积累过氧化损伤。
text.en: Most mesophyll regions may retain better function, whereas localized regions may accumulate peroxidative damage.

text.zh: 相对于干旱对照，叶片衰退可能仍较缓慢。
text.en: Relative to drought controls, leaf decline may still proceed more slowly.

text.zh: 长期结局尤其依赖铁储存、螯合与再分配能力。
text.en: The longer-term outcome particularly depends on iron storage, chelation, and redistribution capacity.

summary.zh: 中期可观察到叶片功能保持与氧化压力之间的可测权衡，而非单向增益。
summary.en: By mid-course, a measurable trade-off between leaf-function preservation and oxidative pressure is expected rather than a unidirectional gain.

text.zh: 部分细胞维持较高的抗氧化和光合能力，另一部分可能出现 ROS 与膜脂过氧化升高。
text.en: Some cells may maintain higher antioxidant and photosynthetic capacity, while others may show increased ROS and membrane-lipid peroxidation.

text.zh: 叶片平均 PSII 效率可能优于对照，但空间异质性和斑驳损伤可能增加。
text.en: Mean leaf PSII efficiency may exceed controls, but spatial heterogeneity and patchy injury may increase.

text.zh: 整株生长保护可能可见，但不会完全抵消干旱导致的生长抑制。
text.en: Protection of whole-plant growth may become visible, but it will not fully offset drought-induced growth inhibition.

text.zh: 叶片平均读数可能掩盖细胞间相反反应。
text.en: Leaf-average readouts may mask opposing responses among cells.

summary.zh: 持续胁迫下，铁稳态调节能力决定 FRO6 上调是维持叶功能还是转为氧化代价。
summary.en: Under sustained stress, iron-homeostasis capacity determines whether FRO6 upregulation maintains leaf function or shifts toward an oxidative cost.

text.zh: 若铁被螯合和隔离，细胞代谢可维持；若未被缓冲，氧化损伤信号将增强。
text.en: If iron is chelated and sequestered, cellular metabolism can be maintained; if not buffered, oxidative-damage signaling will strengthen.

text.zh: 叶绿体完整性可能呈区域性差异，老叶或高光暴露区更易受损。
text.en: Chloroplast integrity may become regionally variable, with older leaves or high-light zones more susceptible to damage.

text.zh: 叶片保持绿色和生物量的优势可能趋于稳定或开始缩小。
text.en: Any advantage in green-leaf retention and biomass may stabilize or begin to narrow.

text.zh: 发育叶龄和实际干旱强度尚未指定，限制后期预测。
text.en: Leaf developmental age and actual drought intensity are unspecified, limiting late-stage prediction.

summary.zh: 至此，气孔限制和水分亏缺主导整株表现；FRO6 更可能调节叶片耐受阈值而非恢复生长。
summary.en: By this point, stomatal limitation and water deficit dominate whole-plant performance; FRO6 is more likely to modulate the leaf tolerance threshold than restore growth.

text.zh: 铁支持的保护代谢与 ROS 相关损伤修复并行，净细胞活力因微环境而异。
text.en: Iron-supported protective metabolism and ROS-associated damage repair proceed in parallel, with net cell viability varying by microenvironment.

text.zh: 叶片可能维持部分光合活性，但气孔和非气孔限制均更明显。
text.en: Leaves may retain partial photosynthetic activity, but both stomatal and non-stomatal limitations become more pronounced.

text.zh: 与干旱对照相比，萎蔫和衰老可能延后，但整株生长仍受抑。
text.en: Wilting and senescence may be delayed versus drought controls, but whole-plant growth remains suppressed.

text.zh: 保护与代价的相对大小无法仅由上调状态确定。
text.en: The relative magnitude of protection and cost cannot be determined from upregulation status alone.

summary.zh: 后期轨迹预计出现耐受性收益递减；未受控的铁还原增强可能暴露氧化脆弱性。
summary.en: The late trajectory is expected to show diminishing tolerance benefits; uncontrolled enhancement of iron reduction may expose oxidative vulnerability.

text.zh: 抗氧化缓冲若耗竭，ROS 与膜损伤将上升；缓冲充足时细胞仍可维持。
text.en: If antioxidant buffering is exhausted, ROS and membrane damage will rise; with adequate buffering, cells can remain viable.

text.zh: 叶片功能差异扩大，部分区域保持绿色而其他区域趋向早衰。
text.en: Functional differences across the leaf widen, with some regions remaining green while others trend toward premature senescence.

text.zh: 相对耐旱表型可能存在，但其幅度小且易受环境波动影响。
text.en: A relative drought-tolerance phenotype may persist, but its magnitude is small and sensitive to environmental variation.

text.zh: 后期预测依赖未提供的铁储量、光环境和表达持续时间。
text.en: Late-stage prediction depends on unprovided iron reserves, light environment, and duration of expression.

summary.zh: 72 小时内最合理的假设是有限且情境依赖的叶片保护，并伴随潜在氧化代价，而非普遍性干旱抗性。
summary.en: At 72 h, the most plausible hypothesis is limited, context-dependent leaf protection accompanied by potential oxidative cost, not universal drought resistance.

text.zh: FRO6 上调可维持部分铁依赖功能，但也可能使高游离铁细胞更易氧化受损。
text.en: FRO6 upregulation can sustain some iron-dependent functions, but may also make cells with high labile iron more prone to oxidative injury.

text.zh: 叶肉平均功能或略优于干旱对照，但叶片内铁和 ROS 分布预计更不均一。
text.en: Average mesophyll function may be slightly better than in drought controls, but within-leaf iron and ROS distributions are expected to be more heterogeneous.

text.zh: 整株仍表现为受旱生长抑制；潜在收益主要是较慢的叶功能衰退。
text.en: The whole plant still shows drought-suppressed growth; the potential benefit is chiefly slower loss of leaf function.

text.zh: 这是叶片情境下的机制假设，不可外推为其他器官或所有基因型的结论。
text.en: This is a mechanistic hypothesis for the leaf context and should not be generalized to other organs or all genotypes.

mechanisms.zh: FRO6 上调可能增强叶片细胞中的铁还原/可利用铁供给，从而影响铁依赖电子传递和酶金属化。
mechanisms.en: FRO6 upregulation may enhance iron reduction or accessible iron supply in leaf cells, affecting iron-dependent electron transfer and enzyme metallation.

mechanisms.zh: 中度干旱诱导 ABA、Ca²⁺和 ROS 信号；铁可用性增加既可能支持抗氧化能力，也可能放大铁介导氧化反应。
mechanisms.en: Moderate drought induces ABA, Ca²⁺, and ROS signaling; increased iron availability may support antioxidant capacity while also amplifying iron-mediated oxidation.

mechanisms.zh: 叶片层面的净效应取决于铁螯合、液泡/细胞器隔离、再分配及光合负荷能否限制游离铁。
mechanisms.en: The net leaf-level effect depends on whether chelation, vacuolar/organelle sequestration, redistribution, and photosynthetic load can constrain labile iron.

assumptions.zh: FRO6 上调发生在营养叶、尤其是叶肉相关细胞中，且表达幅度适中而非极端过表达。
assumptions.en: FRO6 upregulation occurs in vegetative leaves, particularly mesophyll-relevant cells, and is moderate rather than extreme overexpression.

assumptions.zh: 干旱维持在中等强度，植株初始铁营养充足且未伴随盐、病原或严重高光胁迫。
assumptions.en: Drought remains moderate, with adequate initial iron nutrition and no concurrent salinity, pathogen, or severe high-light stress.

assumptions.zh: FRO6 功能向非叶片或非叶肉情境的可迁移性有限；在此类情境下应使用高不确定性。
assumptions.en: Transferability of FRO6 function to non-leaf or non-mesophyll contexts is limited; high uncertainty should be used in such contexts.

assumptions.zh: 方向均相对于同龄、同处理但未上调 FRO6 的植株。
assumptions.en: All directions are relative to age-matched plants under the same treatment without FRO6 upregulation.

readouts.zh: 在 6、12、18 和 24 h 测定 FRO6 转录/蛋白、铁还原活性、可利用铁、ROS、ABA 反应及铁稳态和抗氧化基因表达。
readouts.en: At 6, 12, 18, and 24 h, measure FRO6 transcript/protein, ferric-reduction activity, labile iron, ROS, ABA responses, and iron-homeostasis and antioxidant gene expression.

readouts.zh: 在 24、36、48 和 72 h 测定叶绿素、Fv/Fm 或其他叶绿素荧光参数、气体交换、叶片相对含水量及膜脂过氧化指标。
readouts.en: At 24, 36, 48, and 72 h, measure chlorophyll, Fv/Fm or other chlorophyll-fluorescence parameters, gas exchange, leaf relative water content, and membrane-lipid peroxidation markers.

readouts.zh: 在 36、48 和 72 h 进行叶片铁与 ROS 的空间成像，并结合叶肉细胞特异读数以检验组织异质性。
readouts.en: At 36, 48, and 72 h, spatially image leaf iron and ROS and pair this with mesophyll-specific readouts to test tissue heterogeneity.

readouts.zh: 记录叶片失绿、衰老、叶面积、鲜/干生物量和存活/恢复生长，以区分叶功能保护与真正的整株耐旱。
readouts.en: Record leaf chlorosis, senescence, leaf area, fresh/dry biomass, and survival or recovery growth to distinguish leaf-function protection from true whole-plant drought tolerance.

design.zh: 采用同龄野生型或空载体对照、FRO6 上调材料，以及在可行时的功能缺失/抑制对照；设置充分浇水和中度干旱条件，并监测土壤含水量、叶温和光照。比较叶片与根的响应，不将叶片结果直接外推至其他器官。
design.en: Use age-matched wild-type or empty-vector controls, FRO6-upregulated material, and, where feasible, a loss-of-function or inhibition control; include well-watered and moderate-drought conditions while monitoring soil water content, leaf temperature, and light. Compare leaf and root responses, and do not directly extrapolate leaf results to other organs.

title.zh: 拟南芥根在渐进中度干旱下的72小时条件轨迹
title.en: 72-hour conditional trajectory of Arabidopsis roots under progressive moderate drought

summary.zh: 早期水分亏缺感知启动，尚未预期出现明显根系形态重塑。
summary.en: Early water-deficit sensing begins, before appreciable root architectural remodeling is expected.

text.zh: 细胞水势下降；ABA、Ca²⁺和ROS信号增强，同时膨压驱动的扩张受限。
text.en: Cell water potential falls; ABA, Ca²⁺, and ROS signaling rise while turgor-driven expansion is constrained.

text.zh: 根表皮和皮层的径向水流驱动力减弱，组织尺度变化仍较小。
text.en: The radial water-flow driving force in epidermis and cortex weakens, with tissue-scale changes still small.

text.zh: 主根伸长速率开始下降；总根系构型基本保持。
text.en: Primary-root elongation begins to slow; overall root architecture remains largely unchanged.

text.zh: 根部FRO6相关推断向叶/叶肉已知语境的可迁移性有限；此处主要是通用干旱生理假设。
text.en: Transferability of FRO6-related inference from leaf/mesophyll contexts to roots is limited; this is chiefly a general drought-physiology hypothesis.

summary.zh: 渗透与激素信号持续整合，根细胞开始进入节水和抗氧化状态。
summary.en: Osmotic and hormonal signals continue to integrate, shifting root cells toward water conservation and redox protection.

text.zh: ABA响应、渗透保护物合成和抗氧化防御倾向增强，细胞周期进入受抑准备状态。
text.en: ABA responses, osmoprotectant synthesis, and antioxidant defenses tend to rise, while cells prepare for cell-cycle restraint.

text.zh: 伸长区细胞扩张受限，根尖轴向生长的组织贡献下降。
text.en: Cell expansion in the elongation zone is restricted, reducing its contribution to axial root growth.

text.zh: 根长增量进一步减少，但尚未必然改变侧根数量。
text.en: Incremental root length declines further, without a necessary change in lateral-root number yet.

text.zh: 处理强度和根区实际水势未给定；FRO6在根中的功能关联仍属有限外推。
text.en: Treatment intensity and root-zone water potential are unspecified; functional linkage of FRO6 in roots remains a limited extrapolation.

summary.zh: 转录和代谢重编程应变得可检测，并将资源从生长转向维持。
summary.en: Transcriptional and metabolic reprogramming should become detectable, reallocating resources from growth to maintenance.

text.zh: 胁迫响应转录、离子稳态和清除ROS活动增强，而DNA复制与细胞扩张程序下降。
text.en: Stress-responsive transcription, ion homeostasis, and ROS detoxification rise, whereas DNA replication and cell-expansion programs decline.

text.zh: 根尖分生组织输出和伸长区生长梯度减弱。
text.en: Meristematic output and the growth gradient across the elongation zone weaken.

text.zh: 主根生长持续放缓，根冠至伸长区的长度比例可能缩短。
text.en: Primary-root growth continues to slow, and the root-cap-to-elongation-zone proportion may shorten.

text.zh: 转录响应的时序受基因型、基质和实际失水速率影响；根部FRO6解释不确定。
text.en: Response timing depends on genotype, substrate, and actual dehydration rate; a root-specific FRO6 interpretation is uncertain.

summary.zh: 生长抑制从细胞层面传递至根尖组织，根系开始出现适应性分配。
summary.en: Growth inhibition propagates from cells to root-tip tissues, and adaptive allocation begins in the root system.

text.zh: 细胞分裂和壁松弛活性下降；渗透调节维持剩余膨压。
text.en: Cell division and wall-loosening activity decline; osmotic adjustment helps retain residual turgor.

text.zh: 根尖伸长区缩小，但部分成熟区可能增强内皮层屏障和水分保持相关状态。
text.en: The root-tip elongation zone contracts, while parts of the mature zone may enhance endodermal barrier and water-retention states.

text.zh: 主根长度和根系扩张低于同龄未处理植株。
text.en: Primary-root length and root-system expansion fall below those of age-matched untreated plants.

text.zh: 内皮层屏障的幅度和方向依赖土壤条件；叶/叶肉FRO6知识向根部转用有限。
text.en: The magnitude and direction of endodermal barrier changes depend on soil conditions; transfer of leaf/mesophyll FRO6 knowledge to roots is limited.

summary.zh: 持续中度干旱下，根系进入生长—保水权衡更明显的阶段。
summary.en: Under sustained moderate drought, the root system enters a stage with a clearer growth-versus-water-retention trade-off.

text.zh: 兼容溶质积累、蛋白质稳态和抗氧化能力倾向升高，以支持细胞存活。
text.en: Compatible-solute accumulation, proteostasis, and antioxidant capacity tend to increase to support cell survival.

text.zh: 根尖生长区活性降低，而成熟根段的屏障形成和径向运输调节可能增强。
text.en: Root-tip growth-zone activity declines, whereas barrier formation and radial-transport regulation in mature segments may increase.

text.zh: 根系生物量积累下降；是否出现相对更深的取水配置取决于水分梯度。
text.en: Root biomass accumulation declines; whether relatively deeper water-foraging develops depends on the water gradient.

text.zh: 均匀干旱与垂直水分梯度会导致不同根系构型；FRO6根部作用缺乏直接语境。
text.en: Uniform drought and vertical moisture gradients can produce different root architectures; FRO6 action in roots lacks direct contextual support.

summary.zh: 适应性稳态逐步建立，但持续失水使根尖增殖和扩张继续受压。
summary.en: An adaptive steady state gradually forms, but continued water loss keeps root-tip proliferation and expansion under pressure.

text.zh: 防御和渗透调节维持较高水平，能量消耗型生物合成与细胞周期活动受限。
text.en: Defense and osmotic adjustment remain elevated, while energy-intensive biosynthesis and cell-cycle activity are constrained.

text.zh: 根尖分生组织与伸长区的有效生长容量下降，组织含水状态降低。
text.en: Effective growth capacity of the meristem and elongation zone declines, along with tissue hydration.

text.zh: 主根和侧根的总生长速率低于对照，根系体积扩张受限。
text.en: Combined primary- and lateral-root growth is lower than control, limiting root-system volume expansion.

text.zh: 中度干旱是否保持在可适应范围取决于盆土和蒸散负荷；FRO6外推仍有限。
text.en: Whether moderate drought remains within an acclimatory range depends on pot water status and evaporative demand; FRO6 extrapolation remains limited.

summary.zh: 根部重塑信号可影响侧根发生和成熟区水分运输，但方向具有环境依赖性。
summary.en: Root remodeling signals can affect lateral-root development and water transport in mature zones, but their direction is environment-dependent.

text.zh: 局部细胞可维持渗透调节与存活，而侧根原基启动和伸长相关细胞活动常受抑。
text.en: Local cells may sustain osmotic adjustment and survival, while activities supporting lateral-root initiation and elongation are often restrained.

text.zh: 成熟根的选择性水分通路调节可能增强，同时新生侧根的组织形成可能减少。
text.en: Selective water-pathway regulation in mature roots may increase, while formation of new lateral-root tissues may decrease.

text.zh: 总根生长下降；在存在深层湿润区时，根系空间分配可能向较湿区域偏移。
text.en: Total root growth decreases; if deeper moist zones exist, spatial allocation may shift toward wetter regions.

text.zh: 侧根响应高度依赖水分空间格局和营养供应；根部FRO6推断的可迁移性低。
text.en: Lateral-root responses strongly depend on spatial water pattern and nutrient supply; transferability of root FRO6 inference is low.

summary.zh: 持续胁迫将生长限制固化为可见的根系表型，同时维持防御性代谢。
summary.en: Sustained stress consolidates growth limitation into a visible root phenotype while defensive metabolism is maintained.

text.zh: 胁迫保护性代谢物和抗氧化酶活性预计维持升高，部分细胞进入低生长状态。
text.en: Stress-protective metabolites and antioxidant enzyme activity are expected to remain elevated, with some cells entering a low-growth state.

text.zh: 根尖组织更新与伸长减慢，整体根组织水力通量倾向降低。
text.en: Root-tip tissue renewal and elongation slow, and whole-root hydraulic flux tends to decline.

text.zh: 根长、根表面积和新生侧根贡献预计均低于对照。
text.en: Root length, surface area, and contribution from new lateral roots are expected to be below control.

text.zh: 可见表型受容器大小和根系受限程度影响；不应将其归因于根部FRO6。
text.en: Visible phenotypes depend on container size and root restriction; they should not be attributed specifically to root FRO6.

summary.zh: 若干旱稳定维持，根系可能表现为受限生长下的持续存活与保水。
summary.en: If drought remains stable, the root system may display sustained survival and water conservation under restricted growth.

text.zh: 存活相关的渗透、氧化还原和膜稳态得到维持，但合成代谢与分裂能力继续偏低。
text.en: Survival-related osmotic, redox, and membrane homeostasis are maintained, but anabolic metabolism and proliferative capacity remain low.

text.zh: 成熟组织的保水屏障可能维持，而根尖生长组织继续缩减。
text.en: Water-retention barriers in mature tissues may persist, while root-tip growth tissues continue to contract.

text.zh: 根系增长曲线趋于平缓，根/冠资源分配可能相对偏向维持而非新建组织。
text.en: The root-growth curve flattens, and root/shoot resource allocation may shift toward maintenance rather than new tissue construction.

text.zh: 根/冠分配还受叶片蒸散和碳同化影响；叶肉相关FRO6模型不宜直接用于根。
text.en: Root/shoot allocation also depends on leaf transpiration and carbon assimilation; a mesophyll-related FRO6 model should not be directly applied to roots.

summary.zh: 根部可维持部分驯化稳态，但累积水分限制开始增加损伤风险。
summary.en: Roots may maintain partial acclimatory homeostasis, but cumulative water limitation raises the risk of damage.

text.zh: 保护性反应仍活跃，但持续ROS负荷和能量不足可能使部分细胞功能下降。
text.en: Protective responses remain active, but persistent ROS burden and energy limitation may reduce function in some cells.

text.zh: 根尖生长活性和组织水力输导能力总体偏低，局部组织异质性增加。
text.en: Root-tip growth activity and tissue hydraulic conductance are generally lower, with increased local heterogeneity.

text.zh: 根系扩张继续落后于对照，吸水能力可能限制整株生长。
text.en: Root-system expansion continues to lag behind control, and water uptake capacity may constrain whole-plant growth.

text.zh: 损伤阈值取决于实际基质含水量；FRO6在根内是否参与该阶段尚不明确。
text.en: Damage thresholds depend on actual substrate water content; whether FRO6 participates in this root stage is unclear.

summary.zh: 未复水条件下，生长抑制占主导，适应性保护与损伤累积并存。
summary.en: Without rewatering, growth inhibition dominates, with adaptive protection coexisting with accumulating damage.

text.zh: 仍存活细胞维持渗透和抗氧化防御，但部分根尖细胞可能出现膜完整性下降。
text.en: Surviving cells maintain osmotic and antioxidant defenses, but some root-tip cells may show reduced membrane integrity.

text.zh: 成熟根段可保留功能性水分屏障，而敏感根尖区域可能出现活性斑块化下降。
text.en: Mature root segments may retain functional water barriers, while sensitive root-tip regions may show patchy loss of activity.

text.zh: 净根生长接近停滞或显著降低，根系功能主要转向维持。
text.en: Net root growth approaches arrest or becomes strongly reduced, with root function shifting mainly toward maintenance.

text.zh: 根尖损伤并非中度干旱的必然结果；其发生取决于累积水分亏缺，且FRO6关联不确定。
text.en: Root-tip damage is not inevitable under moderate drought; it depends on cumulative water deficit, and any FRO6 association is uncertain.

summary.zh: 72小时终点预计呈现受抑的根系生长、持续的胁迫保护和环境依赖的结构调整。
summary.en: At 72 h, the expected endpoint is suppressed root growth, persistent stress protection, and environmentally contingent structural adjustment.

text.zh: 渗透调节和防御状态仍高于对照，但增殖、扩张和部分细胞活力低于对照。
text.en: Osmotic adjustment and defense states remain above control, whereas proliferation, expansion, and some cellular viability are below control.

text.zh: 成熟区的屏障性调节可能保留，根尖生长组织缩小且水力功能受限。
text.en: Barrier regulation in mature zones may be retained, while root-tip growth tissues are smaller and hydraulic function is constrained.

text.zh: 相对同龄对照，根长、表面积和生物量积累降低；深层取水偏移仅在存在水分梯度时可能出现。
text.en: Relative to age-matched controls, root length, surface area, and biomass accumulation are reduced; deeper water-foraging shifts are possible only when a moisture gradient exists.

text.zh: 该终点是条件性假设，不代表已验证结果；根部FRO6的有限可迁移性是主要不确定性。
text.en: This endpoint is a conditional hypothesis, not a validated result; limited transferability of FRO6 to roots is a major uncertainty.

mechanisms.zh: 渐进失水降低根细胞水势和膨压，并通过ABA、Ca²⁺与ROS网络优先抑制细胞扩张和分裂。
mechanisms.en: Progressive water loss lowers root-cell water potential and turgor, with ABA, Ca²⁺, and ROS networks preferentially restraining cell expansion and division.

mechanisms.zh: 渗透调节、抗氧化和膜稳态可支持存活，但其碳与能量代价会限制根系新生组织。
mechanisms.en: Osmotic adjustment, antioxidant defense, and membrane homeostasis can support survival, but their carbon and energy costs limit new root tissue.

mechanisms.zh: 成熟根的内皮层屏障和水通道调节可改变径向水流；其净效应受水分空间格局影响。
mechanisms.en: Endodermal barriers and water-channel regulation in mature roots can alter radial water flow; their net effect depends on the spatial water pattern.

mechanisms.zh: FRO6的常见功能语境偏向叶/叶肉；将其机制映射至拟南芥根部干旱反应的可迁移性有限。
mechanisms.en: The common functional context for FRO6 is leaf/mesophyll oriented; mapping its mechanism onto Arabidopsis root drought responses has limited transferability.

assumptions.zh: 干旱在72小时内渐进且维持中度，未发生复水、盐胁迫、严重缺氧或病原胁迫。
assumptions.en: Drought progresses and remains moderate over 72 h, without rewatering, salinity, severe hypoxia, or pathogen stress.

assumptions.zh: 植株为营养生长期、同一遗传背景且根系未受严重盆限；处理组与同龄未处理对照比较。
assumptions.en: Plants are vegetative, of one genetic background, and not severely pot-bound; treated plants are compared with age-matched untreated controls.

assumptions.zh: 若目标是FRO6，本预测仅将其作为根部语境不足时的探索性变量，不能推断根中特异因果作用。
assumptions.en: If FRO6 is the target, this forecast treats it as an exploratory variable under insufficient root context and cannot infer a root-specific causal role.

readouts.zh: 根区基质含水量、叶和根水势、根系水力导度与蒸腾速率。
readouts.en: Root-zone substrate water content, leaf and root water potential, root hydraulic conductance, and transpiration.

readouts.zh: 根尖ABA、ROS、Ca²⁺相关报告信号，以及渗透调节物和脂质过氧化指标。
readouts.en: ABA-, ROS-, and Ca²⁺-related reporter signals in root tips, plus osmolytes and lipid-peroxidation markers.

readouts.zh: 根尖分生组织大小、EdU标记细胞增殖、伸长区长度、细胞膨压或细胞长度。
readouts.en: Root meristem size, EdU-labeled cell proliferation, elongation-zone length, and cell turgor or cell length.

readouts.zh: 主根长度、侧根密度、根表面积、根系生物量及分层取水或根系深度分布。
readouts.en: Primary-root length, lateral-root density, root surface area, root biomass, and stratified water uptake or root-depth distribution.

readouts.zh: 若评估FRO6，检测根与叶中的FRO6转录本或蛋白丰度，并与缺失/过表达材料及互补材料比较。
readouts.en: If evaluating FRO6, measure FRO6 transcripts or protein in roots and leaves, and compare loss-/overexpression lines with complemented material.

design.zh: 设置充分浇水的同龄对照，并在6、12、24、36、48、60和72小时取样；记录实际基质水势。采用至少两个独立批次，区分均匀干旱与具有深层湿润区的水分梯度。若测试FRO6，先确认其在根中的表达与定位，再解释遗传材料的干旱表型。
design.en: Use age-matched well-watered controls and sample at 6, 12, 24, 36, 48, 60, and 72 h while recording actual substrate water status. Include at least two independent batches and distinguish uniform drought from a moisture gradient with a deeper wet zone. If testing FRO6, first establish its root expression and localization before interpreting drought phenotypes of genetic material.

title.zh: 拟南芥根系在中度干旱后复水的恢复轨迹假设
title.en: Hypothesized root recovery trajectory after rewatering following moderate drought in Arabidopsis

summary.zh: 复水首先解除根部水分限制，快速水力恢复与应激信号重设先于可见生长恢复。
summary.en: Rewatering first relieves water limitation in roots; rapid hydraulic recovery and stress-signal resetting precede visible growth recovery.

text.zh: 根细胞膨压和液泡含水状态回升；部分渗透保护物仍维持较高水平。
text.en: Root-cell turgor and vacuolar hydration rise, while some osmoprotectants remain elevated.

text.zh: 根的导水状态改善，皮层和维管组织的水势梯度趋于缓和。
text.en: Root hydraulic status improves, and water-potential gradients across cortex and vascular tissues relax.

text.zh: 主根伸长通常尚未明显恢复；瞬时吸水增加未必转化为净生物量增长。
text.en: Primary-root elongation is usually not yet visibly restored; transient water uptake need not become net biomass gain.

text.zh: 中度干旱的实际持续时间和复水均匀性会影响早期恢复速度。
text.en: The actual duration of moderate drought and uniformity of rewatering can alter early recovery speed.

summary.zh: 水分状态持续恢复，同时ABA主导的干旱应答开始衰减，但转录和代谢重设仍在进行。
summary.en: Water status continues to recover as ABA-dominated drought responses begin to decline, while transcriptional and metabolic resetting remains active.

text.zh: 水合和细胞扩张能力增强，但ROS清除、离子再平衡及渗透物回调并行发生。
text.en: Hydration and expansion capacity improve, while ROS detoxification, ion rebalancing, and osmolyte adjustment proceed concurrently.

text.zh: 根尖及成熟区的水分供应改善，木质部运输恢复更连续。
text.en: Water supply to root tips and mature zones improves, and xylem transport becomes more continuous.

text.zh: 根系生长表型多仍处于滞后期，侧根启动不会立即反映为可见分枝。
text.en: Root-growth phenotypes are largely still in a lag phase; lateral-root initiation will not immediately appear as visible branching.

text.zh: 恢复期激素下降的幅度取决于干旱造成的预处理强度。
text.en: The magnitude of hormonal decline depends on the severity of prior drought conditioning.

summary.zh: 细胞代谢由保守性抗旱状态转向恢复性生长准备，根尖分裂与伸长区逐步解除抑制。
summary.en: Cell metabolism shifts from a conservative drought state toward recovery-oriented growth preparation, gradually releasing inhibition in meristematic and elongation zones.

text.zh: 蛋白合成、糖利用和细胞壁松弛相关活动预计增强，细胞周期再进入增加。
text.en: Protein synthesis, sugar utilization, cell-wall loosening activity, and cell-cycle re-entry are expected to increase.

text.zh: 根尖生长区恢复活性，而受旱较重区域仍可能保留结构和氧化胁迫痕迹。
text.en: Root-tip growth zones regain activity, whereas more drought-affected regions may retain structural and oxidative-stress imprints.

text.zh: 主根日增长速率可开始回升，但通常仍低于同龄未处理植株。
text.en: Daily primary-root growth rate may begin to rise, but is commonly still below that of age-matched untreated plants.

text.zh: 根尖在干旱中受损程度决定生长恢复的起点。
text.en: The extent of drought injury at the root tip determines the onset of growth recovery.

summary.zh: 复水约一天后，水力与基础代谢恢复可支持可测的根生长反弹，但恢复并非干旱过程的简单逆转。
summary.en: About one day after rewatering, hydraulic and basal metabolic recovery can support measurable root-growth rebound, but recovery is not a simple reversal of drought.

text.zh: 细胞伸长、分裂和膜转运活性提高；持续的胁迫记忆相关调控可能仍存在。
text.en: Cell elongation, division, and membrane transport increase; regulation associated with residual stress memory may persist.

text.zh: 根尖分生组织和伸长区的组织级生长活动增强，径向水分分配更稳定。
text.en: Tissue-level growth activity increases in meristematic and elongation zones, with more stable radial water distribution.

text.zh: 主根伸长和细根形成潜力上升，但总根长仍可能低于未处理对照。
text.en: Primary-root elongation and fine-root formation potential rise, although total root length may remain below untreated controls.

text.zh: 若复水后盆土短暂缺氧，生长反弹可能被削弱。
text.en: If rewatering transiently causes soil hypoxia, the growth rebound may be weakened.

summary.zh: 恢复性碳分配逐渐加强，根系可将部分资源从防御维持转向生长和吸收功能。
summary.en: Recovery-oriented carbon allocation gradually strengthens, allowing roots to shift some resources from defensive maintenance toward growth and uptake.

text.zh: 糖代谢、线粒体供能和养分转运能力预计继续提高。
text.en: Sugar metabolism, mitochondrial energy supply, and nutrient-transport capacity are expected to continue increasing.

text.zh: 成熟区吸收表面及维管连接功能改善，根毛区可能逐步恢复活性。
text.en: Absorptive-surface and vascular-connection function improve in mature zones, and the root-hair zone may progressively regain activity.

text.zh: 根系吸水和矿质吸收能力提升，主根增长趋于持续。
text.en: Root water and mineral uptake capacity improve, and primary-root growth becomes more sustained.

text.zh: 营养供给充足的设定支持该预测；低养分会改变碳—养分分配。
text.en: This forecast assumes adequate nutrition; low nutrient availability would alter carbon–nutrient allocation.

summary.zh: 根系进入较稳定的恢复阶段，持续生长与组织重塑并行，先前干旱造成的空间异质性仍可保留。
summary.en: Roots enter a more stable recovery phase in which sustained growth and tissue remodeling proceed together, while spatial heterogeneity from prior drought can remain.

text.zh: 多数生长细胞活性增强，但部分细胞继续执行抗氧化、修复和自噬清除程序。
text.en: Activity rises in most growth-associated cells, while some cells continue antioxidant, repair, and autophagic-clearance programs.

text.zh: 根尖生长带扩大，受损或较老区域的组织修复和导水整合继续进行。
text.en: The root-tip growth zone expands, while tissue repair and hydraulic integration continue in damaged or older regions.

text.zh: 根长增加应更易检测到；侧根原基的后续发育可能开始贡献根系结构恢复。
text.en: Increasing root length should be more readily detectable; later development of lateral-root primordia may begin contributing to architectural recovery.

text.zh: 侧根响应对干旱史和根系发育阶段尤其敏感。
text.en: Lateral-root responses are especially sensitive to drought history and root developmental stage.

summary.zh: 水分与养分获取趋于协调，根系从急性恢复转入功能重建阶段。
summary.en: Water and nutrient acquisition become increasingly coordinated as roots transition from acute recovery to functional rebuilding.

text.zh: 离子稳态和膜电化学梯度更接近正常，生长相关基因表达预计占比提高。
text.en: Ion homeostasis and membrane electrochemical gradients approach normality, and growth-associated expression is expected to become more prominent.

text.zh: 维管组织供给与皮层吸收之间的耦合增强，根毛功能更稳定。
text.en: Coupling between vascular supply and cortical uptake strengthens, and root-hair function becomes more stable.

text.zh: 根系功能恢复可支持地上部重新获得水分，但整株形态补偿尚不充分。
text.en: Recovered root function can support renewed shoot hydration, but whole-plant morphological compensation remains incomplete.

text.zh: 根毛恢复是否显著取决于复水时根毛区的存活和基质条件。
text.en: Whether root-hair recovery is prominent depends on survival of the root-hair zone and substrate conditions during rewatering.

summary.zh: 两天内，根部主要水力限制预计基本解除，但先前干旱形成的发育和资源分配后果仍持续。
summary.en: Within two days, major root hydraulic limitation is expected to be largely relieved, but developmental and allocation consequences of prior drought persist.

text.zh: 细胞含水和基础能量状态接近稳态；修复和生长程序达到较平衡的共存状态。
text.en: Cell hydration and basal energy status approach steady state; repair and growth programs reach a more balanced coexistence.

text.zh: 根系水力连续性和吸收组织功能接近恢复平台，局部重塑仍在继续。
text.en: Root hydraulic continuity and absorptive-tissue function approach a recovery plateau, while local remodeling continues.

text.zh: 主根延长持续，侧根出芽或伸长可能使根系分枝度开始上升。
text.en: Primary-root extension continues, and lateral-root emergence or elongation may begin increasing root branching.

text.zh: 恢复平台不代表与未处理植株完全等同，尤其是累积根长和分枝格局。
text.en: A recovery plateau does not imply full equivalence to untreated plants, especially for cumulative root length and branching pattern.

summary.zh: 根系可表现出有限的补偿性生长，但其幅度受干旱记忆、碳储备和发育窗口共同约束。
summary.en: Roots may display limited compensatory growth, but its extent is jointly constrained by drought memory, carbon reserves, and developmental windows.

text.zh: 在活跃根尖，细胞增殖和扩张保持较高；在成熟区，细胞活动更偏向运输和维持。
text.en: In active root tips, cell proliferation and expansion remain elevated; in mature zones, activity is oriented more toward transport and maintenance.

text.zh: 新生侧根和根尖组织增长，而较早受旱区的结构限制可能持续存在。
text.en: New lateral roots and root-tip tissues grow, whereas structural constraints in earlier drought-affected zones may persist.

text.zh: 总根长和有效吸收面积继续增加，但能否超过对照的即时增长速率尚不确定。
text.en: Total root length and effective absorptive area continue to increase, but whether instantaneous growth exceeds controls remains uncertain.

text.zh: 补偿性生长高度依赖基因型、盆栽空间和干旱前的根系构型。
text.en: Compensatory growth strongly depends on genotype, pot space, and pre-drought root architecture.

summary.zh: 恢复后的根系逐步建立新的功能稳态，其资源分配可能与未经历干旱的植株不同。
summary.en: Recovered roots progressively establish a new functional steady state whose resource allocation may differ from plants that did not experience drought.

text.zh: 细胞水分、氧化还原和能量状态总体稳定，残余胁迫记忆可维持选择性转录差异。
text.en: Cellular water, redox, and energy states are broadly stable, while residual stress memory may maintain selective transcriptional differences.

text.zh: 根部导水与吸收网络趋于稳定，新生组织继续整合入既有根系。
text.en: Root hydraulic and uptake networks become more stable, while new tissues continue integrating into the existing root system.

text.zh: 根系仍在生长，但相对未处理植株的差距更可能表现为累积性而非急性功能缺陷。
text.en: Roots continue growing, but divergence from untreated plants is more likely to appear as a cumulative deficit than as an acute functional impairment.

text.zh: 未指定基因型和容器限制，限制了对长期根构型的判断。
text.en: Unspecified genotype and container constraints limit inference about longer-term root architecture.

summary.zh: 根系恢复主要体现为持续组织更新和构型调整，而非进一步显著的急性水力变化。
summary.en: Root recovery is expressed mainly as continued tissue renewal and architectural adjustment rather than further major acute hydraulic change.

text.zh: 生长区细胞维持正常化的分裂和伸长节律，修复负担进一步降低。
text.en: Cells in growth zones maintain increasingly normalized division and elongation rhythms, with a further reduced repair burden.

text.zh: 侧根发育与根毛更新可继续增加功能吸收面积，维管整合保持稳定。
text.en: Lateral-root development and root-hair renewal can continue increasing functional absorptive area, while vascular integration remains stable.

text.zh: 根系构型朝恢复方向发展，但总规模和空间分布可能仍未追平同龄对照。
text.en: Root architecture continues recovering, but overall scale and spatial distribution may still not match age-matched controls.

text.zh: 构型恢复的时间尺度常超过72小时，因此此时的外推有限。
text.en: Architectural recovery often extends beyond 72 hours, limiting inference at this horizon.

summary.zh: 三天后，根的急性复水反应应大体结束，留下的差异主要是干旱史塑造的生长与构型后果。
summary.en: After three days, acute root responses to rewatering should be largely complete; remaining differences are chiefly growth and architectural consequences shaped by drought history.

text.zh: 细胞层面接近新的稳态，但部分胁迫记忆和代谢配置差异可能持续。
text.en: At the cellular scale, roots approach a new steady state, though some stress memory and metabolic-allocation differences may persist.

text.zh: 组织水力和吸收功能总体稳定；新生根段与旧根段之间仍可能存在恢复不均。
text.en: Tissue hydraulic and uptake functions are broadly stable; recovery may remain uneven between newly formed and older root segments.

text.zh: 即时根生长可接近正常，但累计根长、侧根密度和根系空间格局仍可能低于或不同于对照。
text.en: Immediate root growth may approach normal, but cumulative root length, lateral-root density, and spatial architecture may still be lower than or different from controls.

text.zh: 该轨迹适用于所述中度干旱；更强或更久的干旱可导致更慢或不完全恢复。
text.en: This trajectory applies to the stated moderate drought; stronger or longer drought may cause slower or incomplete recovery.

mechanisms.zh: 复水提高根—土水势差可用性和细胞膨压，先恢复水力与膜运输，再支持细胞扩张和分裂。
mechanisms.en: Rewatering restores available root–soil water-potential gradients and cell turgor, recovering hydraulics and membrane transport before supporting expansion and division.

mechanisms.zh: ABA、ROS、钙信号及渗透调节网络在复水后逐步重设；其衰减通常慢于水分状态的物理恢复。
mechanisms.en: ABA, ROS, calcium signaling, and osmotic-regulation networks are progressively reset after rewatering; their decline commonly lags physical restoration of water status.

mechanisms.zh: 干旱后的碳分配、根尖损伤、侧根发育窗口和局部组织记忆，使恢复呈现滞后、空间不均和非逆转性。
mechanisms.en: Post-drought carbon allocation, root-tip injury, lateral-root developmental windows, and local tissue memory make recovery delayed, spatially uneven, and non-reversible.

assumptions.zh: 假定为营养充足、未受其他胁迫的营养生长期拟南芥，且复水能均匀恢复根际水分而不造成持续积水缺氧。
assumptions.en: Assumes vegetative Arabidopsis with adequate nutrition and no other stress, and rewatering that uniformly restores rhizosphere moisture without sustained waterlogging or hypoxia.

assumptions.zh: 方向均相对于每个时间点同龄、未处理植株；“增加”不表示必然超过对照绝对值。
assumptions.en: Directions are relative to age-matched untreated plants at each timepoint; “increase” does not mean values necessarily exceed control absolute levels.

assumptions.zh: 未提供基因型、干旱持续时间、基质和容器大小，因此对侧根补偿和长期构型的置信度降低。
assumptions.en: Genotype, drought duration, substrate, and container size are unspecified, reducing confidence for lateral-root compensation and long-term architecture.

assumptions.zh: 未指定FRO6扰动；FRO6若被作为解释变量，其叶片/叶肉相关功能向根部情境的可迁移性有限，应采用高不确定性。
assumptions.en: No FRO6 perturbation is specified; if FRO6 is used as an explanatory variable, transferability of its leaf/mesophyll-related function to roots is limited and should carry high uncertainty.

readouts.zh: 在6、12、24、48和72小时测定根和叶水势、相对含水量、根系液压导度或吸水速率。
readouts.en: Measure root and leaf water potential, relative water content, root hydraulic conductance, or water-uptake rate at 6, 12, 24, 48, and 72 h.

readouts.zh: 测定根部ABA、可溶性糖、脯氨酸、ROS/氧化还原指标及关键离子含量，以区分水合恢复与应激重设。
readouts.en: Measure root ABA, soluble sugars, proline, ROS/redox markers, and key ion contents to distinguish hydration recovery from stress resetting.

readouts.zh: 量化根尖分生组织大小、EdU标记细胞增殖、细胞长度、根毛密度以及主根和侧根的时序生长。
