mechanisms.en: Under drought, increased reduced iron may support iron-dependent photosynthetic, respiratory, and antioxidant processes, but may also increase ROS risk through iron-catalyzed reactions.

mechanisms.zh: 螯合、转运、液泡隔离和过氧化物清除的容量决定铁动员最终表现为适应还是损伤。
mechanisms.en: The capacities for chelation, transport, vacuolar sequestration, and peroxide removal determine whether iron mobilization ultimately manifests as adaptation or damage.

assumptions.zh: FRO6上调在处理期间持续，并产生具有功能意义的蛋白或还原活性增加。
assumptions.en: FRO6 upregulation persists during treatment and produces a functionally meaningful increase in protein abundance or reductase activity.

assumptions.zh: 中度干旱造成生理性水分限制但未导致广泛不可逆组织死亡，养分供应保持充足。
assumptions.en: Moderate drought imposes physiological water limitation without widespread irreversible tissue death, while nutrient supply remains adequate.

assumptions.zh: 预测主要借鉴叶片/叶肉相关铁氧化还原逻辑；对完整幼苗、根部或其他非叶肉细胞的转移性有限。
assumptions.en: The forecast mainly draws on leaf/mesophyll-related iron-redox logic; transferability to intact seedlings, roots, or other non-mesophyll cells is limited.

assumptions.zh: 未指定上调剂量、遗传背景及细胞类型，因此所有方向均为相对同龄未处理植物或相应干旱对照的条件性预期。
assumptions.en: Upregulation dose, genetic background, and cell type are unspecified; all directions are conditional expectations relative to age-matched untreated plants or corresponding drought controls.

readouts.zh: 在6、18、36和72 h测定FRO6转录/蛋白水平及根或叶表面Fe(III)还原活性。
readouts.en: Measure FRO6 transcript/protein abundance and root- or leaf-surface Fe(III)-reduction activity at 6, 18, 36, and 72 h.

readouts.zh: 测定Fe(II)/Fe(III)比例、可交换铁池、铁螯合或储存相关标志，以及空间铁分布。
readouts.en: Measure Fe(II)/Fe(III) ratio, labile iron pool, iron-chelation or storage markers, and spatial iron distribution.

readouts.zh: 在18、36、54和72 h检测ROS、脂质过氧化、抗氧化酶活性、叶绿素和光系统II效率。
readouts.en: At 18, 36, 54, and 72 h, assay ROS, lipid peroxidation, antioxidant-enzyme activity, chlorophyll, and photosystem II efficiency.

readouts.zh: 测定叶片相对含水量、气孔导度、叶温、叶面积、根冠比和幼苗生物量。
readouts.en: Measure leaf relative water content, stomatal conductance, leaf temperature, leaf area, root-to-shoot ratio, and seedling biomass.

design.zh: 采用同龄野生型或空载体对照，并设置充分浇水和中度干旱条件下的FRO6上调与非上调组；尽可能加入叶肉特异与非叶肉表达比较。按6–72 h连续取样，并将铁形态、ROS和生理性状与表达强度关联分析。
design.en: Use age-matched wild-type or empty-vector controls, with FRO6-upregulated and non-upregulated groups under well-watered and moderate-drought conditions; where possible, compare mesophyll-specific with non-mesophyll expression. Sample serially from 6 to 72 h and relate iron speciation, ROS, and physiological traits to expression magnitude.

title.zh: 水稻叶片在渐进性中度干旱下的72小时条件轨迹
title.en: Conditional 72-hour trajectory of rice leaves under progressive moderate drought

summary.zh: 早期失水感知启动，叶片尚主要表现为可逆的节水反应。
summary.en: Early water-deficit sensing begins, with predominantly reversible water-saving responses in the leaf.

text.zh: ABA、Ca²⁺和活性氧信号增强；保卫细胞离子外流开始，渗透调节尚处早期。
text.en: ABA, Ca²⁺, and reactive-oxygen signaling increase; guard-cell ion efflux begins, while osmotic adjustment remains early.

text.zh: 叶片水势与气孔导度轻度下降，维管束至叶肉的水分供应开始受限。
text.en: Leaf water potential and stomatal conductance decline slightly, and water supply from vasculature to mesophyll begins to be constrained.

text.zh: 蒸腾和气体交换略降；肉眼可见的叶片形态变化通常不明显。
text.en: Transpiration and gas exchange decline modestly; visible leaf morphological change is usually minimal.

text.zh: 早期信号反应具有较强普适性，但起始速度取决于土壤含水量和蒸散需求。
text.en: Early signaling is broadly supported, but onset depends on soil water status and evaporative demand.

summary.zh: ABA主导的气孔调控加强，并开始诱导抗旱相关转录重编程。
summary.en: ABA-dominated stomatal regulation strengthens and begins to induce drought-associated transcriptional reprogramming.

text.zh: 脱水响应转录、抗氧化酶防御和相容性溶质合成倾向上升；光合电子传递调节增强。
text.en: Dehydration-response transcription, antioxidant defense, and compatible-solute synthesis tend to rise; regulation of photosynthetic electron transport increases.

text.zh: 表皮气孔开度下降，叶肉细胞膨压开始降低，但组织完整性大多保持。
text.en: Epidermal stomatal aperture declines and mesophyll turgor begins to fall, while tissue integrity is largely maintained.

text.zh: 气孔限制使净同化速率和蒸腾进一步下降，叶温可能相对升高。
text.en: Stomatal limitation further reduces net assimilation and transpiration, potentially raising leaf temperature.

text.zh: 该阶段取决于根区干旱向叶片传递的程度及空气湿度。
text.en: This stage depends on the degree of root-zone drought transmission to the leaf and on air humidity.

summary.zh: 节水与细胞保护程序并行，光合碳固定开始受到更明确限制。
summary.en: Water conservation and cellular protection proceed in parallel, while photosynthetic carbon fixation becomes more clearly constrained.

text.zh: 可溶性糖、脯氨酸及抗氧化能力上升；叶绿体CO₂同化和部分蛋白合成趋于下降。
text.en: Soluble sugars, proline, and antioxidant capacity increase; chloroplastic CO₂ assimilation and some protein synthesis tend to decline.

text.zh: 叶肉细胞间隙CO₂供应减少，叶片水分梯度加大，局部叶肉膨压下降。
text.en: Intercellular CO₂ supply to mesophyll decreases, leaf water gradients increase, and local mesophyll turgor declines.

text.zh: 净光合速率下降更明显，叶片扩张和新生叶生长开始受抑。
text.en: Net photosynthesis declines more clearly, and leaf expansion and growth of newly emerging leaves begin to be restrained.

text.zh: 渗透调节的幅度受品种、叶龄和既有碳储备影响。
text.en: The magnitude of osmotic adjustment is affected by cultivar, leaf age, and pre-existing carbon reserves.

summary.zh: 在持续中度胁迫下，气孔限制与初始非气孔限制共同压低叶片碳收益。
summary.en: Under sustained moderate stress, stomatal limitation and emerging non-stomatal limitation jointly reduce leaf carbon gain.

text.zh: 保护性代谢与ROS清除维持升高；叶绿体能量利用受限，光抑制风险开始增加。
text.en: Protective metabolism and ROS scavenging remain elevated; chloroplast energy use is constrained and photoinhibition risk begins to rise.

text.zh: 气孔关闭加深、叶肉水分状态下降；仍存活组织通过渗透调节维持部分水合。
text.en: Stomatal closure deepens and mesophyll water status declines; surviving tissue retains partial hydration through osmotic adjustment.

text.zh: CO₂吸收、蒸腾和叶片生长均下降，轻微叶片卷曲或叶角改变可能出现。
text.en: CO₂ uptake, transpiration, and leaf growth decline; slight leaf rolling or altered leaf angle may emerge.

text.zh: 是否出现可见卷叶取决于处理强度、叶位和昼夜蒸散波动。
text.en: Whether visible rolling occurs depends on treatment intensity, leaf position, and day–night variation in evaporative demand.

summary.zh: 持续脱水使资源从生长转向维持、修复和水分保存。
summary.en: Sustained dehydration shifts resources from growth toward maintenance, repair, and water conservation.

text.zh: 脱水保护蛋白、渗透调节物和抗氧化网络持续积累；蛋白质周转增强。
text.en: Dehydration-protective proteins, osmolytes, and antioxidant networks continue to accumulate; protein turnover increases.

text.zh: 叶片导水能力与叶肉膨压进一步降低，叶脉邻近区域可能优先维持水分。
text.en: Leaf hydraulic conductance and mesophyll turgor decline further, while regions near veins may preferentially retain water.

text.zh: 叶片日间气体交换持续受限，生物量积累速率低于同龄未处理植株。
text.en: Daytime leaf gas exchange remains constrained, and biomass accumulation is slower than in age-matched untreated plants.

text.zh: 中度干旱下的空间水分分配具有组织异质性，预测为概念性趋势。
text.en: Spatial water allocation under moderate drought is tissue-heterogeneous; this is a conceptual trend prediction.

summary.zh: 适应性防御仍在运行，但累积碳限制开始削弱长期维持能力。
summary.en: Adaptive defenses remain active, but cumulative carbon limitation begins to weaken long-term maintenance capacity.

text.zh: 抗氧化与渗透保护维持较高水平；糖代谢重分配和叶绿体修复需求增加。
text.en: Antioxidant and osmoprotective activities remain high; sugar-metabolism reallocation and chloroplast repair demand increase.

text.zh: 叶肉光合能力和组织含水量继续下降，表皮—叶肉间的水分与CO₂扩散限制加重。
text.en: Mesophyll photosynthetic capacity and tissue hydration continue to decline, with stronger epidermis–mesophyll constraints on water and CO₂ diffusion.

text.zh: 叶片光合效率和生长表现降低；在高蒸散时段，卷叶更可能可见。
text.en: Leaf photosynthetic efficiency and growth performance decrease; rolling becomes more likely during periods of high evaporative demand.

text.zh: 昼夜恢复程度会显著影响此时的表型强度。
text.en: The extent of day–night recovery will strongly affect phenotypic severity at this point.

summary.zh: 叶片进入稳态胁迫维持阶段，防御代价与光合损失同时累积。
summary.en: The leaf enters a sustained stress-maintenance phase in which defense costs and photosynthetic losses accumulate together.

text.zh: 应激转录和清除ROS的能力保持升高；部分叶绿体色素与光系统修复效率可能下降。
text.en: Stress transcription and ROS-scavenging capacity remain elevated; some chloroplast pigment status and photosystem repair efficiency may decline.

text.zh: 叶片相对含水量、气孔导度和叶肉CO₂利用能力均保持低于对照。
text.en: Leaf relative water content, stomatal conductance, and mesophyll CO₂-use capacity remain below control levels.

text.zh: 净碳收益持续偏低，叶片扩张受限；老叶可能先出现轻度失绿倾向。
text.en: Net carbon gain remains low and leaf expansion is constrained; older leaves may first show a mild tendency toward chlorosis.

text.zh: 失绿是否发生取决于叶龄及实际胁迫是否仍属中度。
text.en: Whether chlorosis occurs depends on leaf age and whether the applied stress remains moderate.

summary.zh: 水分保存继续优先于生产，非气孔性光合限制的贡献可能增加。
summary.en: Water conservation continues to take priority over productivity, and non-stomatal contributions to photosynthetic limitation may increase.

text.zh: 渗透调节物和防御代谢维持升高；持续ROS负荷可能降低部分光合酶活性。
text.en: Osmolytes and defense metabolism remain elevated; persistent ROS burden may reduce activity of some photosynthetic enzymes.

text.zh: 叶肉细胞水合与光合组织功能进一步受限，但在中度胁迫下应仍以可逆变化为主。
text.en: Mesophyll hydration and photosynthetic-tissue function are further constrained, but changes should remain mainly reversible under moderate stress.

text.zh: 叶片CO₂同化、蒸腾冷却和生长持续降低，日间叶温升高的可能性增加。
text.en: Leaf CO₂ assimilation, transpirational cooling, and growth remain reduced, increasing the likelihood of elevated daytime leaf temperature.

text.zh: 非气孔限制的相对重要性需以荧光和气体交换联合判定。
text.en: The relative importance of non-stomatal limitation requires joint fluorescence and gas-exchange assessment.

summary.zh: 若干保护过程接近新的稳态，但叶片可用于生长的碳和水资源持续不足。
summary.en: Several protective processes approach a new steady state, while carbon and water available for growth remain persistently insufficient.

text.zh: 细胞保护与修复持续消耗能量；光合产物输出和细胞扩张相关代谢趋于降低。
text.en: Cellular protection and repair continue to consume energy; photosynthate export and metabolism associated with cell expansion tend to decline.

text.zh: 叶片水力状态低于对照，叶肉与表皮细胞扩张受限，组织生长梯度减弱。
text.en: Leaf hydraulic status remains below control, mesophyll and epidermal cell expansion are constrained, and tissue growth gradients weaken.

text.zh: 叶面积增长和日尺度碳积累下降；卷叶可在高需水时段反复出现。
text.en: Leaf-area growth and daily carbon accumulation decline; rolling may recur during periods of high water demand.

text.zh: 预测假定未发生严重萎蔫或不可逆组织损伤。
text.en: This prediction assumes that severe wilting or irreversible tissue damage has not occurred.

summary.zh: 持续干旱使防御维持与组织功能衰退之间的权衡更加突出。
summary.en: Continued drought accentuates the trade-off between maintaining defenses and declining tissue function.

text.zh: 抗氧化和脱水响应仍被维持；若修复不足，膜脂过氧化与光系统损伤风险上升。
text.en: Antioxidant and dehydration responses are maintained; if repair is insufficient, risks of lipid peroxidation and photosystem damage rise.

text.zh: 叶片含水、叶绿体功能和气孔—叶肉协同调节进一步偏离对照。
text.en: Leaf hydration, chloroplast function, and coordinated stomatal–mesophyll regulation diverge further from the control.

text.zh: 光合表现和叶片生长持续降低，较老叶片的衰老或失绿风险上升。
text.en: Photosynthetic performance and leaf growth remain reduced, with increasing risk of senescence or chlorosis in older leaves.

text.zh: 后期结果对土壤水分阈值及品种耐旱性尤其敏感。
text.en: Later outcomes are particularly sensitive to soil-water threshold and cultivar drought tolerance.

summary.zh: 在未缓解的中度干旱下，叶片维持低耗水、低碳同化状态，并可能出现早期衰老分化。
summary.en: Under unrelieved moderate drought, the leaf maintains a low-water-use, low-carbon-assimilation state and may show incipient senescence heterogeneity.

text.zh: 保护基因表达和渗透调节持续；部分细胞的光合代谢下降，老叶细胞更易累积氧化负荷。
text.en: Protective gene expression and osmotic adjustment persist; photosynthetic metabolism declines in some cells, while older-leaf cells more readily accumulate oxidative burden.

text.zh: 叶脉邻近或较年轻区域可能相对受保护，而老叶叶尖、叶缘更易出现水分与光合功能下降。
text.en: Vein-adjacent or younger regions may be relatively protected, whereas tips and margins of older leaves are more prone to declines in hydration and photosynthetic function.

text.zh: 整体叶片功能仍低于对照，卷叶、叶温升高或局部失绿的可见概率增加。
text.en: Overall leaf function remains below control, with increasing likelihood of visible rolling, higher leaf temperature, or localized chlorosis.

text.zh: 组织分区和衰老程度高度依赖叶龄、基因型及实际水分亏缺。
text.en: Tissue partitioning and senescence severity depend strongly on leaf age, genotype, and actual water deficit.

summary.zh: 预测终点为适应性节水与累积功能损失并存；是否保持可逆取决于实际水分状态。
summary.en: The predicted endpoint combines adaptive water saving with accumulated functional loss; reversibility depends on actual water status.

text.zh: 渗透保护和抗氧化反应仍高于对照；光合修复能力、膜稳定性和细胞生长代谢可能下降。
text.en: Osmoprotective and antioxidant responses remain above control; photosynthetic repair capacity, membrane stability, and cellular growth metabolism may decline.

text.zh: 叶片整体水合和光合组织活性降低，但中度处理下部分区域可在复水后恢复。
text.en: Whole-leaf hydration and photosynthetic-tissue activity are reduced, but some regions may recover after rewatering under moderate treatment.

text.zh: 相对对照，叶片气体交换、碳积累和扩张生长均降低，并可能伴随卷叶或轻度失绿。
text.en: Relative to the control, leaf gas exchange, carbon accumulation, and expansion growth are reduced, potentially with rolling or mild chlorosis.

text.zh: 72小时后的可逆性不能仅由“中度干旱”判定，需量化土壤与叶片水分状态。
text.en: Reversibility after 72 h cannot be inferred from “moderate drought” alone; soil and leaf water status must be quantified.

mechanisms.zh: 根区水分下降经水力信号及ABA相关信号传至叶片，促进保卫细胞失水和气孔关闭。
mechanisms.en: Declining root-zone water status is transmitted to leaves through hydraulic and ABA-related signals, promoting guard-cell water loss and stomatal closure.

mechanisms.zh: 气孔关闭先限制CO₂供应；胁迫持续后，叶绿体代谢、光系统修复和氧化还原平衡也会限制同化。
mechanisms.en: Stomatal closure first limits CO₂ supply; with sustained stress, chloroplast metabolism, photosystem repair, and redox balance also constrain assimilation.

mechanisms.zh: 渗透调节物、抗氧化系统和脱水保护蛋白有助于维持细胞水合与膜稳定，但会消耗原本可用于生长的资源。
mechanisms.en: Osmolytes, antioxidant systems, and dehydration-protective proteins help preserve cellular hydration and membrane stability, but consume resources otherwise available for growth.

mechanisms.zh: 叶龄、叶脉距离和局部蒸散差异可造成叶片内水分状态、光合损失和衰老风险的空间异质性。
mechanisms.en: Leaf age, distance from veins, and local transpiration differences can generate spatial heterogeneity in water status, photosynthetic loss, and senescence risk.

assumptions.zh: 处理确为渐进、持续的中度干旱，未达到快速致死性失水，且不发生复水。
assumptions.en: The treatment is genuinely progressive and sustained moderate drought, without rapid lethal dehydration or rewatering.

assumptions.zh: 植株为营养生长期、养分充足的水稻，预测针对受处理叶片而非根、穗或全株产量。
assumptions.en: Plants are adequately nourished vegetative-stage rice; predictions concern treated leaves rather than roots, panicles, or whole-plant yield.

assumptions.zh: 标准光周期下，日间高蒸散会放大气孔关闭、叶温升高和卷叶，而夜间可能出现部分水分恢复。
assumptions.en: Under a standard photoperiod, daytime evaporative demand amplifies stomatal closure, leaf warming, and rolling, whereas partial overnight water recovery may occur.

assumptions.zh: 未指定基因型、叶龄、盆土性质或实际水分阈值，因此后期组织损伤与可逆性的置信度较低。
assumptions.en: Genotype, leaf age, soil properties, and actual water-deficit thresholds are unspecified, reducing confidence in late tissue damage and reversibility.

readouts.zh: 土壤含水量、基质水势、叶水势、相对含水量和渗透势。
readouts.en: Soil water content, substrate water potential, leaf water potential, relative water content, and osmotic potential.

readouts.zh: 气孔导度、蒸腾、净光合速率、胞间CO₂浓度及叶温的昼夜连续测量。
readouts.en: Diurnal measurements of stomatal conductance, transpiration, net photosynthesis, intercellular CO₂ concentration, and leaf temperature.

readouts.zh: 叶绿素荧光与色素指标，用于区分气孔限制和光化学/非气孔限制。
readouts.en: Chlorophyll fluorescence and pigment metrics to distinguish stomatal from photochemical/non-stomatal limitation.

readouts.zh: ABA、可溶性糖、脯氨酸、ROS/脂质过氧化及抗氧化酶活性。
readouts.en: ABA, soluble sugars, proline, ROS/lipid peroxidation, and antioxidant-enzyme activity.

readouts.zh: 叶片卷曲评分、叶面积扩张、失绿空间分布，以及复水后的恢复轨迹。
readouts.en: Leaf-rolling score, leaf-area expansion, spatial chlorosis pattern, and recovery trajectory after rewatering.

design.zh: 设置同龄、同叶位、充分灌溉对照，并记录实际土壤与叶片水分状态；在6、12、24、36、48、60和72小时取样，气体交换和叶温应覆盖昼夜。加入72小时复水组以检验预测变化的可逆性，并按叶龄分层分析。
design.en: Include age- and leaf-position-matched well-watered controls and record actual soil and leaf water status; sample at 6, 12, 24, 36, 48, 60, and 72 h, while capturing diurnal gas exchange and leaf temperature. Add a 72-h rewatering group to test reversibility and stratify analyses by leaf age.

title.zh: 水稻叶片中度干旱后复水的72小时恢复轨迹假设
title.en: Hypothesized 72-hour recovery trajectory after rewatering moderately droughted rice leaves

summary.zh: 复水首先缓解叶片水分亏缺，但恢复初期仍存在干旱遗留的气孔与氧化还原限制。
summary.en: Rewatering first relieves leaf water deficit, while drought legacy effects on stomata and redox state remain early in recovery.

text.zh: 叶肉细胞水势和膨压迅速回升；残余ABA信号、ROS清除负荷及膜运输重调仍偏离未处理叶片。
text.en: Mesophyll water potential and turgor rebound rapidly; residual ABA signaling, ROS-detoxification demand, and membrane-transport adjustment still differ from untreated leaves.

text.zh: 叶片水力状态改善，但气孔导度与蒸腾通常仍低于同龄未处理叶片。
text.en: Leaf hydraulic status improves, but stomatal conductance and transpiration are typically still below age-matched untreated leaves.

text.zh: 全株即时碳同化恢复受限，干旱期间损失的生长量尚未补回。
text.en: Whole-plant instantaneous carbon gain remains constrained, and growth lost during drought has not yet been replaced.

text.zh: 复水后水力恢复通常较快，但恢复速率受品种、盆土与干旱历时影响。
text.en: Hydraulic recovery after rewatering is commonly rapid, but its rate depends on cultivar, soil conditions, and drought duration.

summary.zh: 水分供应趋于稳定，气孔重开与光合电子传递恢复开始不同步。
summary.en: Water supply becomes more stable, while stomatal reopening and recovery of photosynthetic electron transport begin to proceed asynchronously.

text.zh: 渗透调节物质逐步回调，叶绿体电子传递压力下降；部分抗氧化和胁迫转录响应仍可维持。
text.en: Osmotic adjustment begins to reset and chloroplast electron-transport pressure declines; some antioxidant and stress-transcriptional responses may persist.

text.zh: 叶片气孔开放趋于恢复，但气孔斑块性和较低的叶肉导度仍可能限制CO₂扩散。
text.en: Leaf stomata trend toward reopening, but stomatal patchiness and lower mesophyll conductance may still limit CO₂ diffusion.

text.zh: 净光合恢复早于可见生长恢复，植株碳收支仍可能低于未处理对照。
text.en: Net photosynthesis recovers before visible growth, and plant carbon balance may remain below the untreated control.

text.zh: 昼夜相位会显著改变气孔与光合读数的表观恢复。
text.en: Diurnal phase can substantially alter the apparent recovery of stomatal and photosynthetic readouts.

summary.zh: 从快速水力恢复转向代谢与转录重建，复水并不意味着所有干旱响应立即消失。
summary.en: Recovery shifts from rapid hydraulics to metabolic and transcriptional rebuilding; rewatering does not imply immediate loss of all drought responses.

text.zh: 离子稳态、糖代谢和蛋白质周转正在重建；部分保护性代谢物和应激标记仍高于未处理水平。
text.en: Ion homeostasis, sugar metabolism, and protein turnover are being rebuilt; some protective metabolites and stress markers remain above untreated levels.

text.zh: 叶脉供水已较充分，但表皮气孔与叶肉光合能力的恢复程度可能不一致。
text.en: Vein water supply is largely restored, but epidermal stomata and mesophyll photosynthetic capacity may recover to different extents.

text.zh: 叶片碳固定可明显改善，但全株叶面积形成和生物量积累仍落后于未受旱植株。
text.en: Leaf carbon fixation may improve markedly, but whole-plant leaf-area formation and biomass accumulation still lag undroughted plants.

text.zh: 若干旱在夜间结束或复水跨越暗期，糖代谢轨迹会不同。
text.en: If drought ends at night or rewatering spans a dark period, the sugar-metabolic trajectory may differ.

summary.zh: 经历一个日周期后，叶片功能性恢复更易观察到，但干旱造成的发育性损失开始显现。
summary.en: After one diel cycle, functional leaf recovery is easier to detect, while drought-induced developmental losses become apparent.

text.zh: 叶绿体能量平衡接近正常，ABA降解与信号衰减继续进行；修复相关转录与蛋白更新仍未完全结束。
text.en: Chloroplast energy balance approaches normal and ABA degradation/signaling attenuation continue; repair-associated transcription and protein renewal are not yet complete.

text.zh: 多数区域的气体交换接近对照，但先前受损或发育较老区域可能保留较低光合效率。
text.en: Gas exchange in most regions approaches control levels, but previously stressed or older regions may retain lower photosynthetic efficiency.

text.zh: 当日碳输入改善，但已错过的叶片扩展窗口和分蘖资源投入未必能完全逆转。
text.en: Daily carbon input improves, but missed windows for leaf expansion and tiller resource investment may not be fully reversible.

text.zh: 对叶龄和受旱时长的缺乏信息限制了对不可逆损伤的判断。
text.en: Missing information on leaf age and drought duration limits inference about irreversible damage.

summary.zh: 复水诱导的恢复程序与残留胁迫记忆并存，叶片内部恢复可能呈空间异质性。
summary.en: Rewatering-induced recovery programs coexist with residual stress memory, and recovery within the leaf may be spatially heterogeneous.

text.zh: 水分与离子状态大多正常化，但防御、抗氧化和表观调控相关状态可能仍保留部分干旱记忆。
text.en: Water and ion status are largely normalized, but defense, antioxidant, and epigenetic-regulatory states may retain aspects of drought memory.

text.zh: 整体气孔导度接近对照，局部叶区仍可能因水力或光合恢复差异而表现不均。
text.en: Bulk stomatal conductance approaches control, while local leaf zones may remain uneven because hydraulic and photosynthetic recovery differs spatially.

text.zh: 植株资源分配开始重新支持生长，但累积生物量缺口仍持续存在。
text.en: Plant resource allocation begins to support growth again, but the accumulated biomass deficit persists.

text.zh: 是否出现空间异质性取决于叶龄、蒸散负荷和根系复水均一性。
text.en: Whether spatial heterogeneity occurs depends on leaf age, evaporative demand, and uniformity of root-zone rewetting.

summary.zh: 恢复进入整合阶段：碳代谢逐渐支持修复和新生长，而非仅维持胁迫防护。
summary.en: Recovery enters an integration phase: carbon metabolism increasingly supports repair and new growth rather than only stress protection.

text.zh: 多数细胞水分状态、离子梯度和基础能量代谢预计接近未处理水平。
text.en: Water status, ion gradients, and basal energy metabolism in most cells are expected to be near untreated levels.

text.zh: 叶片平均光合与气孔功能可接近对照，但叶肉限制或老叶损伤可能仍造成小幅残余偏差。
text.en: Mean leaf photosynthesis and stomatal function may approach control, though mesophyll limitation or damage in older leaves can leave modest residual deviations.

text.zh: 新同化物可重新投入生长，但全株相对于未受旱对照的累计大小仍较小。
text.en: New assimilate can again be invested in growth, but cumulative plant size remains smaller than in undroughted controls.

text.zh: 此阶段高度依赖复水后是否维持适宜光照、温度和养分供应。
text.en: This phase strongly depends on maintaining suitable light, temperature, and nutrient supply after rewatering.

summary.zh: 叶片功能大体恢复，但恢复程度开始由先前干旱期间的结构性损伤决定。
summary.en: Leaf function is broadly restored, but the degree of recovery is increasingly determined by structural damage incurred during drought.

text.zh: 若未发生显著膜或叶绿体损伤，细胞氧化还原和渗透状态应接近对照。
text.en: If substantial membrane or chloroplast damage did not occur, cellular redox and osmotic status should be close to control.

text.zh: 叶片平均水力和气体交换接近正常；受损细胞群或叶尖边缘可维持局部低效。
text.en: Average leaf hydraulics and gas exchange approach normal; damaged cell populations or leaf tip and margin zones can retain local inefficiency.

text.zh: 全株生长恢复可能加快，但不能自动补偿早期干旱造成的发育延迟。
text.en: Whole-plant growth recovery may accelerate, but it does not automatically compensate for developmental delay caused by earlier drought.

text.zh: “中度干旱”范围未量化，因此难以区分完全可逆与轻微结构性损伤。
text.en: Because “moderate drought” is not quantified, fully reversible effects cannot be cleanly separated from mild structural damage.

summary.zh: 两个日周期内，未受不可逆损伤的叶片可达到近稳态功能；生长轨迹仍保留历史效应。
summary.en: Within two diel cycles, leaves without irreversible injury can reach near-steady function, while the growth trajectory retains historical effects.

text.zh: 基础代谢和水分关系预计稳定在接近对照的范围，诱导型胁迫反应进一步消退。
text.en: Basal metabolism and water relations are expected to stabilize near the control range, with induced stress responses further subsiding.

text.zh: 叶片整体气孔调节、叶温和光化学效率预计大致恢复至未处理范围。
text.en: Bulk leaf stomatal regulation, leaf temperature, and photochemical efficiency are expected to return broadly to the untreated range.

text.zh: 当前生理表现可近正常，但累计叶面积和生物量通常仍低于同龄未处理植株。
text.en: Current physiology can be near normal, but cumulative leaf area and biomass are typically still below age-matched untreated plants.

text.zh: 该预测适用于持续充分复水且未叠加病害、盐分或热胁迫的条件。
text.en: This projection applies to sustained adequate rewatering without superimposed disease, salinity, or heat stress.

summary.zh: 恢复后的稳态逐步建立，决定后续表现的重点由叶片即时生理转向生长补偿能力。
summary.en: A post-rewatering steady state gradually establishes, shifting the determinant of later performance from immediate leaf physiology to growth-compensation capacity.

text.zh: 细胞层面预计仅保留低水平的胁迫记忆或修复性蛋白周转差异。
text.en: At the cellular level, only low-level differences in stress memory or repair-related protein turnover are expected to remain.

text.zh: 叶片尺度的水分与CO₂交换应基本稳定；局部老化受旱区可能例外。
text.en: Leaf-scale water and CO₂ exchange should be largely stable, except potentially in locally aged drought-affected zones.

text.zh: 若未启动显著补偿性分蘖或叶片生长，植株总体大小仍低于对照。
text.en: Unless substantial compensatory tillering or leaf growth is initiated, overall plant size remains below control.

text.zh: 补偿生长取决于品种、源库关系及营养可利用性，当前信息不足。
text.en: Compensatory growth depends on cultivar, source-sink relations, and nutrient availability, which are insufficiently specified.

summary.zh: 叶片从急性恢复转入后恢复适应期，功能差异主要表现为发育和资源分配后果。
summary.en: The leaf transitions from acute recovery to post-recovery adaptation, with remaining differences expressed mainly through development and resource allocation.

text.zh: 在持续适宜环境下，细胞水分、能量和氧化还原稳态预计维持接近对照。
text.en: Under continuing favorable conditions, cellular water, energy, and redox homeostasis are expected to remain near control.

text.zh: 叶片平均光合能力和气孔响应预计保持接近对照，而非持续反弹增强。
text.en: Average leaf photosynthetic capacity and stomatal responsiveness are expected to remain near control rather than show sustained rebound enhancement.

text.zh: 全株累计生长差距仍是主要表型；是否缩小取决于之后的新叶和分蘖生长。
text.en: The cumulative whole-plant growth gap remains the main phenotype; whether it narrows depends on subsequent new-leaf and tiller growth.

text.zh: 缺少根系状态信息，限制了对长期水分吸收与补偿生长的判断。
text.en: Lack of root-status information limits inference about longer-term water uptake and compensatory growth.

summary.zh: 大多数可逆叶片生理限制应已解除，残余影响更可能来自已发生的生长机会损失。
summary.en: Most reversible physiological limitations in the leaf should be resolved; residual effects are more likely to arise from lost growth opportunities.

text.zh: 细胞胁迫响应预计回落至基础状态附近，除非干旱造成隐性光合或膜损伤。
text.en: Cellular stress responses are expected to return near basal state unless drought caused latent photosynthetic or membrane injury.

text.zh: 叶片水力、蒸腾和光化学表现预计与同龄未处理叶片相近。
text.en: Leaf hydraulics, transpiration, and photochemical performance are expected to resemble those of age-matched untreated leaves.

text.zh: 植株仍可能因较小叶面积、较少分蘖或较晚发育而低于对照。
text.en: The plant may still remain below control because of smaller leaf area, fewer tillers, or delayed development.

text.zh: 若复水后出现强光或高温，隐性损伤可能被放大并延长恢复。
text.en: If strong light or high temperature occurs after rewatering, latent damage may be amplified and recovery prolonged.

summary.zh: 72小时后，叶片可接近功能稳态，但全株表现不应被视为干旱前状态的简单时间逆转。
summary.en: By 72 hours, the leaf can approach functional steady state, but whole-plant performance should not be viewed as a simple reversal to the pre-drought state.

text.zh: 可逆的细胞水分与代谢扰动预计基本恢复；残留差异主要指向损伤或胁迫记忆。
text.en: Reversible cellular water and metabolic disturbances are expected to be largely restored; remaining differences mainly indicate damage or stress memory.

text.zh: 叶片整体气体交换和光系统功能可接近对照，但应检验是否存在局部或叶龄相关残余限制。
text.en: Bulk leaf gas exchange and photosystem function may approach control, but local or leaf-age-dependent residual limitations should be tested.

text.zh: 全株累计生长和发育进程预计仍低于未处理植株，后续是否补偿尚不确定。
text.en: Cumulative whole-plant growth and developmental progression are expected to remain below untreated plants; later compensation is uncertain.

text.zh: 对叶片即时恢复的机制支持较强，但对全株补偿的预测受基因型和发育信息限制。
text.en: Mechanistic support for immediate leaf recovery is stronger, whereas prediction of whole-plant compensation is limited by genotype and developmental information.

mechanisms.zh: 复水先恢复根—叶水力连续性和细胞膨压，随后才逐步解除ABA介导的气孔限制。
mechanisms.en: Rewatering first restores root-to-leaf hydraulic continuity and cellular turgor, then more gradually relieves ABA-mediated stomatal limitation.

mechanisms.zh: 光合恢复受气孔CO₂供应、叶肉扩散、叶绿体能量平衡及ROS修复的共同约束，因此可能不同步。
mechanisms.en: Photosynthetic recovery is jointly constrained by stomatal CO₂ supply, mesophyll diffusion, chloroplast energy balance, and ROS repair, and can therefore be asynchronous.

mechanisms.zh: 干旱期间丧失的叶片扩展、分蘖投入和发育时间具有路径依赖性，复水后不一定立即补偿。
mechanisms.en: Drought-induced losses in leaf expansion, tiller investment, and developmental time are path-dependent and may not be immediately compensated after rewatering.

assumptions.zh: 复水均匀且持续充分，根区不存在缺氧、盐分积累或养分限制。
assumptions.en: Rewatering is uniform and continuously adequate, with no root-zone hypoxia, salt accumulation, or nutrient limitation.

assumptions.zh: 中度干旱未造成广泛不可逆的叶片坏死、严重膜损伤或根系死亡。
assumptions.en: Moderate drought did not cause widespread irreversible leaf necrosis, severe membrane injury, or root death.

assumptions.zh: 预测针对营养生长期水稻叶片；品种、叶龄、干旱持续时间和复水发生的昼夜相位尚未指定。
assumptions.en: The projection concerns vegetative rice leaves; cultivar, leaf age, drought duration, and the diel timing of rewatering are unspecified.

readouts.zh: 在复水前及6、12、24、48、72小时测定叶水势、相对含水量、叶温、气孔导度、蒸腾和净光合。
readouts.en: Measure leaf water potential, relative water content, leaf temperature, stomatal conductance, transpiration, and net photosynthesis before rewatering and at 6, 12, 24, 48, and 72 h.

readouts.zh: 测定叶绿素荧光参数、叶绿体氧化还原/ROS指标、ABA及可溶性糖，以区分水力、气孔和代谢限制。
readouts.en: Measure chlorophyll-fluorescence parameters, chloroplast redox/ROS indicators, ABA, and soluble sugars to distinguish hydraulic, stomatal, and metabolic limitations.

readouts.zh: 记录叶面积、新叶出现、分蘖数和干物质量，并将其与即时叶片气体交换分开解释。
readouts.en: Record leaf area, new-leaf emergence, tiller number, and dry mass, and interpret these separately from immediate leaf gas exchange.

design.zh: 设置同龄持续灌水对照、持续干旱对照和复水组；在相同日内时刻重复采样，并记录土壤含水量、光照和温度。可增加不同叶龄分层及根区氧状态监测，以检验局部残余限制与补偿生长。
design.en: Include age-matched continuously watered, continued-drought, and rewatered groups; sample at matched times of day while recording soil water content, light, and temperature. Stratify by leaf age and monitor root-zone oxygen where feasible to test local residual limitation and compensatory growth.

title.zh: 水稻叶片非冻害冷胁迫的72小时条件轨迹
title.en: 72-hour conditional trajectory of non-freezing cold stress in rice leaves

summary.zh: 冷感知与快速信号转导启动，尚无明显叶片表型。
summary.en: Cold perception and rapid signal transduction begin, with little visible leaf phenotype.

text.zh: 膜流动性下降，Ca²⁺、ROS和激酶信号短暂升高；代谢通量开始受限。
text.en: Membrane fluidity declines, while Ca²⁺, ROS, and kinase signals rise transiently; metabolic flux begins to be constrained.

text.zh: 叶肉组织结构大体保持，气孔与维管反应尚不均一。
text.en: Mesophyll structure is broadly retained, with still-heterogeneous stomatal and vascular responses.

text.zh: 肉眼叶色和生长基本不变，光合效率可能仅出现早期波动。
text.en: Visible leaf color and growth remain largely unchanged; photosynthetic efficiency may show early fluctuation.

text.zh: 快速信号反应具有较强普适性，但温度设定未给出。
text.en: Rapid signaling is broadly supported, but the treatment temperature is unspecified.

summary.zh: 冷响应转录重编程扩大，光合与渗透调节开始重新分配资源。
summary.en: Cold-responsive transcriptional reprogramming expands, reallocating resources toward protection and osmotic adjustment.

text.zh: 保护性转录因子、抗氧化和渗透调节相关表达上升；叶绿体电子传递受抑。
text.en: Protective transcription-factor, antioxidant, and osmotic-adjustment programs rise, while chloroplast electron transport is constrained.

text.zh: 保卫细胞倾向降低气孔开放，叶肉同化能力开始下降。
text.en: Guard cells tend toward lower stomatal opening, and mesophyll carbon assimilation begins to decline.

text.zh: 净光合速率与叶片冷却条件下的碳获取预计开始下降。
text.en: Net photosynthesis and leaf carbon gain under cold conditions are expected to begin declining.

text.zh: 响应幅度取决于昼夜阶段及栽培稻耐冷背景。
text.en: Response magnitude depends on diel phase and the cold tolerance background of the cultivar.

summary.zh: 防御与稳态维持持续增强，但能量代价逐渐显现。
summary.en: Defense and homeostatic maintenance continue to strengthen, while their energy cost becomes apparent.

text.zh: 可溶性糖、相容性溶质和清除ROS能力增加；ATP供给与蛋白质折叠压力并存。
text.en: Soluble sugars, compatible solutes, and ROS-scavenging capacity increase; ATP limitation and protein-folding stress coexist.

text.zh: 叶肉细胞膨压和细胞间CO₂供应下降，局部气孔关闭更明显。
text.en: Mesophyll turgor and intercellular CO₂ supply decline, with more evident local stomatal closure.

text.zh: 叶片气体交换受限，生长性碳分配开始被防御性消耗替代。
text.en: Leaf gas exchange is constrained, and carbon allocation for growth begins to be displaced by defensive expenditure.

text.zh: 中等冷胁迫通常可诱导适应，但其阈值随品种而变。
text.en: Moderate cold commonly induces acclimation, but its threshold varies among cultivars.

summary.zh: 经历一个昼夜周期后，适应性调节与光抑制风险共同塑造叶片状态。
summary.en: After one diel cycle, acclimatory regulation and photoinhibition risk jointly shape leaf status.

text.zh: 冷适应相关蛋白和膜脂重塑增加；若光能过剩，叶绿体ROS与PSII压力升高。
text.en: Cold-acclimation proteins and membrane-lipid remodeling increase; if excitation energy is excessive, chloroplast ROS and PSII pressure rise.

text.zh: 叶片整体仍完整，但叶脉附近与叶尖等区域可出现功能异质性。
text.en: The leaf remains structurally intact, but functional heterogeneity may emerge near veins and at leaf tips.

text.zh: 最大光化学效率和CO₂同化通常低于同龄未处理叶片。
text.en: Maximum photochemical efficiency and CO₂ assimilation are typically below those of age-matched untreated leaves.

text.zh: 稳定光照降低光环境混杂，但光强本身未说明。
text.en: Stable light reduces environmental confounding, but its intensity is unspecified.

summary.zh: 持续冷胁迫使保护反应由急性信号转向代谢稳态维持。
summary.en: Continued cold shifts the response from acute signaling toward metabolic homeostasis.

text.zh: 膜脂不饱和化和抗氧化周转持续；糖利用、核糖体活动和细胞扩张受抑。
text.en: Membrane lipid unsaturation and antioxidant turnover continue; sugar use, ribosomal activity, and cell expansion are constrained.

text.zh: 叶肉碳同化与维管输出减慢，组织水分状态略趋保守。
text.en: Mesophyll assimilation and vascular export slow, while tissue water status becomes more conservative.

text.zh: 叶面积扩张和新叶生长速度预计低于未处理植株。
text.en: Leaf-area expansion and new-leaf growth are expected to be slower than in untreated plants.

text.zh: 生长抑制的出现时间受叶龄和根系冷暴露程度影响。
text.en: The timing of growth inhibition depends on leaf age and the extent of root cold exposure.

summary.zh: 适应网络若足够有效，可部分稳定细胞；否则氧化损伤开始累积。
summary.en: If acclimation networks are effective, cells partly stabilize; otherwise, oxidative damage begins to accumulate.

text.zh: 抗氧化酶和伴侣蛋白维持较高活性，同时脂质过氧化风险上升。
text.en: Antioxidant enzymes and chaperones remain active, while the risk of lipid peroxidation rises.

text.zh: 多数叶肉仍具活性，但敏感区域可能出现轻度失水或叶绿体功能不均。
text.en: Most mesophyll remains viable, but sensitive regions may show mild dehydration or uneven chloroplast performance.

text.zh: 日间光合恢复不完全，叶片生物量积累继续放缓。
text.en: Daytime photosynthetic recovery is incomplete, and leaf biomass accumulation continues to slow.

text.zh: 损伤与适应的平衡高度依赖实际冷温度及处理持续性。
text.en: The balance between injury and acclimation strongly depends on actual temperature and treatment persistence.

summary.zh: 资源分配进一步偏向维持与防御，组织功能下降但未必发生不可逆伤害。
summary.en: Resource allocation shifts further toward maintenance and defense; tissue function declines without necessarily becoming irreversible.

text.zh: 应激转录维持，翻译和细胞周期相关活动降低；部分细胞积累保护性糖。
text.en: Stress transcription persists, while translation and cell-cycle-related activity decline; some cells accumulate protective sugars.

text.zh: 气孔导度、叶肉导度和韧皮部装载能力预计整体降低。
text.en: Stomatal conductance, mesophyll conductance, and phloem-loading capacity are expected to decline overall.

text.zh: 叶片蒸腾与同化同步降低，植株相对生长率受抑。
text.en: Leaf transpiration and assimilation decline together, suppressing relative growth rate.

text.zh: 未指定品种耐冷性，限制了对持续功能损失的判断。
text.en: Unspecified cultivar cold tolerance limits inference about persistent functional loss.

summary.zh: 两天冷暴露后，耐冷叶片应表现为低通量稳态，敏感叶片则可能进入损伤累积期。
summary.en: After two days of cold exposure, tolerant leaves may reach a low-flux steady state, whereas sensitive leaves may enter cumulative injury.

text.zh: 稳态保护代谢维持较高水平；敏感背景中ROS、膜损伤和蛋白降解增加。
text.en: Protective metabolism remains elevated; in sensitive backgrounds, ROS, membrane injury, and protein degradation increase.

text.zh: 组织完整性通常仍在，但叶尖或边缘可先出现功能衰退。
text.en: Tissue integrity is usually retained, but functional decline may first appear at tips or margins.

text.zh: 叶片光合能力和扩张均持续低于对照，轻度卷叶风险增加。
text.en: Leaf photosynthetic capacity and expansion remain below control, with increased risk of mild rolling.

text.zh: 耐受与损伤分叉在此阶段更明显，而基因型未知。
text.en: Tolerance-versus-injury divergence becomes more apparent at this stage, but genotype is unknown.

summary.zh: 持续冷胁迫下，叶片进入受限维持状态，生长恢复信号仍受压制。
summary.en: Under continued cold, the leaf enters constrained maintenance, with growth-recovery signals still suppressed.

text.zh: 细胞扩张、蛋白合成和初级代谢能力整体降低，保护代谢消耗持续。
text.en: Cell expansion, protein synthesis, and primary metabolic capacity decline overall, while protective metabolism remains costly.

text.zh: 叶片水力与气孔调节维持保守模式，组织碳输出偏低。
text.en: Leaf hydraulic and stomatal regulation retain a conservative mode, with low tissue carbon export.

text.zh: 可见生长停滞倾向增强，叶色可能开始轻度变暗或失绿。
text.en: Visible growth arrest becomes more likely, and leaves may begin mild darkening or chlorosis.

text.zh: 是否出现可见失绿取决于冷强度与叶片年龄。
text.en: Whether visible chlorosis appears depends on cold severity and leaf age.

summary.zh: 保护能力与慢性损伤之间的差异继续扩大。
summary.en: The divergence between protective capacity and chronic injury continues to widen.

text.zh: 耐冷细胞维持膜与氧化还原稳态；敏感细胞可能出现持续ROS和能量缺口。
text.en: Cold-tolerant cells maintain membrane and redox homeostasis; sensitive cells may show persistent ROS and energy deficits.

text.zh: 耐冷叶片可保持大部分组织活性，敏感叶片的边缘和叶尖功能下降加重。
text.en: Cold-tolerant leaves may retain most tissue activity, whereas functional loss at margins and tips worsens in sensitive leaves.

text.zh: 总体叶片生产力持续下降；敏感植株更可能出现卷叶或失绿。
text.en: Overall leaf productivity remains depressed; sensitive plants are more likely to show rolling or chlorosis.

text.zh: 该阶段的表型分化不能在缺少品种信息时可靠定量。
text.en: Phenotypic divergence at this stage cannot be reliably quantified without cultivar information.

summary.zh: 若冷胁迫不解除，持续低碳输入限制叶片修复与全株生长。
summary.en: If cold stress persists, sustained low carbon input limits leaf repair and whole-plant growth.

text.zh: 碳骨架供应和生物合成进一步受限，受损大分子的更新速度下降。
text.en: Carbon-skeleton supply and biosynthesis become further constrained, slowing renewal of damaged macromolecules.

text.zh: 叶肉功能与源叶输出能力降低，叶片内部代谢异质性增强。
text.en: Mesophyll function and source-leaf export capacity decline, while metabolic heterogeneity within the leaf increases.

text.zh: 叶片对全株生长的支持减弱，处理后恢复潜力可能下降。
text.en: Leaf support for whole-plant growth weakens, and post-treatment recovery potential may decline.

text.zh: 恢复潜力还取决于夜温、根系状态和后续复温条件。
text.en: Recovery potential also depends on night temperature, root status, and subsequent rewarming conditions.

summary.zh: 三天后预计形成“适应性低通量”或“渐进性伤害”两种条件性状态。
summary.en: After three days, the leaf is expected to occupy either an acclimated low-flux state or a progressively injured state.

text.zh: 耐冷背景可维持保护性稳态；敏感背景中膜、叶绿体和蛋白质损伤更可能累积。
text.en: Tolerant backgrounds may maintain protective homeostasis; membrane, chloroplast, and protein injury is more likely to accumulate in sensitive backgrounds.

text.zh: 叶片组织可仍保持存活，但功能区缩小；严重时叶尖边缘先发生坏死。
text.en: Leaf tissue may remain viable but with a reduced functional area; under stronger injury, tip and margin necrosis may occur first.

text.zh: 相对未处理植株，光合、蒸腾、叶扩张和植株生长均预计降低。
text.en: Relative to untreated plants, photosynthesis, transpiration, leaf expansion, and plant growth are all expected to be reduced.

text.zh: 72小时结果对实际温度、品种、叶龄和根冠是否同受冷尤为敏感。
text.en: The 72-hour outcome is particularly sensitive to temperature, cultivar, leaf age, and whether roots and shoots are both chilled.

mechanisms.zh: 低温降低膜流动性并触发Ca²⁺、ROS、激酶和激素相关信号，继而重编程基因表达。
mechanisms.en: Cold lowers membrane fluidity and triggers Ca²⁺, ROS, kinase, and hormone-related signaling, followed by transcriptional reprogramming.

mechanisms.zh: 叶绿体碳同化与电子传递受温度限制；在稳定光照下，吸收光能与利用能力失配可提高光抑制风险。
mechanisms.en: Chloroplast carbon assimilation and electron transport are temperature-limited; under stable light, mismatch between absorbed energy and utilization can raise photoinhibition risk.

mechanisms.zh: 渗透调节物、抗氧化系统、伴侣蛋白和膜脂重塑可促进适应，但会消耗碳和能量并抑制生长。
mechanisms.en: Osmotic adjusters, antioxidant systems, chaperones, and membrane-lipid remodeling can promote acclimation, but consume carbon and energy and suppress growth.

assumptions.zh: 冷处理为非冻害、恒定且中等强度；未发生霜冻、干旱、病害或养分限制。
assumptions.en: Cold treatment is non-freezing, constant, and moderate, with no frost, drought, disease, or nutrient limitation.

assumptions.zh: 预测主要针对受处理的营养生长期叶片；根系是否同时受冷未说明，因此对全株效应的外推有限。
assumptions.en: The forecast primarily concerns treated vegetative leaves; whether roots are also chilled is unspecified, limiting transfer to whole-plant effects.

assumptions.zh: 未给出品种、精确温度、叶龄和光强；这些因素可显著改变适应与伤害的分界。
assumptions.en: Cultivar, exact temperature, leaf age, and light intensity are unspecified; these factors can strongly shift the boundary between acclimation and injury.

readouts.zh: 在6、12、24、48和72小时测定叶温、气孔导度、蒸腾、净光合速率、胞间CO₂和叶绿素荧光参数。
readouts.en: At 6, 12, 24, 48, and 72 h, measure leaf temperature, stomatal conductance, transpiration, net photosynthesis, intercellular CO₂, and chlorophyll-fluorescence parameters.

readouts.zh: 测定电解质渗漏、脂质过氧化、ROS、抗氧化酶活性、可溶性糖和渗透调节物。
readouts.en: Measure electrolyte leakage, lipid peroxidation, ROS, antioxidant-enzyme activity, soluble sugars, and osmotic adjusters.

readouts.zh: 评估叶片含水状态、叶绿素含量、卷叶/失绿评分、叶面积扩张及干物质积累。
readouts.en: Assess leaf water status, chlorophyll content, rolling/chlorosis scores, leaf-area expansion, and dry-matter accumulation.

readouts.zh: 在早期信号与24–72小时适应阶段进行转录或蛋白质组采样，以区分急性信号、适应和损伤。
readouts.en: Collect transcript or protein samples during early signaling and the 24–72 h acclimation phase to distinguish acute signaling, acclimation, and injury.

design.zh: 采用同龄未处理对照、温度相同但遮光或不同光强的对照，以及至少一个耐冷和一个敏感栽培稻背景；同步记录昼夜时间、根区温度和叶龄，并在72小时后设置复温恢复观察。
design.en: Use age-matched untreated controls, controls at the same temperature with shading or altered light intensity, and at least one cold-tolerant and one sensitive rice background; record diel time, root-zone temperature, and leaf age, and include post-72 h rewarming recovery observations.

title.zh: 中度干旱下水稻叶片FRO6上调的条件性轨迹
title.en: Conditional trajectory of FRO6 upregulation in rice leaves under moderate drought

summary.zh: 首先预计出现FRO6相关的细胞铁氧化还原调节，而非可见叶片表型。
summary.en: FRO6-linked cellular iron-redox regulation is expected first, before visible leaf phenotypes.

text.zh: 推定铁(III)还原能力和局部还原态铁供给上升；游离铁池变化仍不确定。
text.en: Putative ferric-reduction capacity and local reduced-iron supply increase; the labile iron pool remains uncertain.

text.zh: 叶片总体铁含量和组织结构预计尚无明显变化。
text.en: Bulk leaf iron content and tissue structure are not expected to change appreciably yet.

text.zh: 叶片卷曲、失水和光合性状预计接近同龄干旱对照。
text.en: Leaf rolling, water loss, and photosynthetic traits should remain near those of age-matched drought controls.

text.zh: 叶片背景提高了可转移性，但水稻FRO6的亚细胞定位、剂量和基因型未知。
text.en: The leaf context improves transferability, but rice FRO6 localization, dosage, and genotype are unspecified.

summary.zh: 铁稳态与干旱氧化还原信号可能开始耦合，并诱导早期防御调节。
summary.en: Iron-homeostasis and drought redox signals may begin to couple, inducing early protective regulation.

text.zh: 还原态铁利用可能增强，同时短暂升高铁驱动活性氧风险；抗氧化响应或被启动。
text.en: Reduced-iron utilization may increase, while iron-driven ROS risk may transiently rise; antioxidant responses may be initiated.

text.zh: 叶肉细胞间的铁再分配可能起始，但尚不足以改变整体叶片状态。
text.en: Iron redistribution among mesophyll cells may begin, but is unlikely to alter bulk leaf status.

text.zh: 气孔行为和叶水势的差异预计仍小且不稳定。
text.en: Differences in stomatal behavior and leaf water status are expected to be small and variable.

text.zh: 预测依赖FRO6确实在叶肉铁还原或铁可利用性中发挥作用。
text.en: This prediction depends on FRO6 functioning in mesophyll iron reduction or iron availability.

summary.zh: 若FRO6提高叶绿体可利用铁，光合电子传递保护与抗氧化基因调节可逐步显现。
summary.en: If FRO6 improves chloroplast-available iron, protection of photosynthetic electron transport and antioxidant regulation may emerge.

text.zh: 铁依赖电子传递和抗氧化酶体系的维持能力推定上升。
text.en: Capacity to sustain iron-dependent electron transport and antioxidant enzyme systems is predicted to increase.

text.zh: 叶肉光合细胞的氧化还原缓冲能力可能提高，组织损伤尚有限。
text.en: Redox-buffering capacity in photosynthetic mesophyll cells may improve while tissue injury remains limited.

text.zh: 净光合和蒸腾的群体尺度差异可能尚未清晰。
text.en: Canopy- or organ-scale differences in net photosynthesis and transpiration may not yet be clear.

text.zh: 效应可能被干旱诱导的气孔关闭所掩盖。
text.en: Drought-induced stomatal closure may mask the effect.

summary.zh: 细胞保护可能转化为较稳定的叶片光合维持，但铁过量还原可形成代价。
summary.en: Cellular protection may translate into more stable leaf photosynthesis, though excessive iron reduction could impose a cost.

text.zh: 叶绿体铁利用和ROS清除可能增强；若可交换铁过高，则脂质过氧化风险也上升。
text.en: Chloroplast iron use and ROS scavenging may improve; if exchangeable iron becomes excessive, lipid-peroxidation risk also rises.

text.zh: 叶绿素-蛋白复合体和叶肉细胞膜完整性的保持倾向增强。
text.en: Retention of chlorophyll-protein complexes and mesophyll membrane integrity tends to improve.

text.zh: 在相同干旱强度下，光合效率和叶片膨压保持可能略优于对照。
text.en: Under equal drought intensity, photosynthetic efficiency and leaf turgor retention may be modestly better than controls.

text.zh: 方向取决于FRO6上调是否改善可利用铁而非扩大有害游离铁池。
text.en: Direction depends on whether FRO6 improves usable iron rather than enlarging a harmful labile iron pool.

summary.zh: 叶片层面的铁分配、光合保护与节水响应可能开始共同决定表型。
summary.en: Leaf iron partitioning, photosynthetic protection, and water-saving responses may jointly begin to determine phenotype.

text.zh: 推定抗氧化还原力和光系统修复能力提高，限制持续氧化损伤。
text.en: Antioxidant reducing power and photosystem repair capacity are predicted to increase, limiting persistent oxidative injury.

text.zh: 叶肉组织中光抑制和早期失绿的发展可能减缓。
text.en: Development of photoinhibition and early chlorosis in mesophyll tissue may slow.

text.zh: 叶片相对含水量和PSII运行效率可能表现出小幅优势。
text.en: Leaf relative water content and PSII operating efficiency may show a modest advantage.

text.zh: 未给出干旱持续方式、FRO6表达幅度或叶片发育叶位。
text.en: Drought regime, magnitude of FRO6 expression, and leaf developmental position are unspecified.

summary.zh: 在持续中度干旱下，保护性铁利用若成立，应在功能叶肉组织中更易检测。
summary.en: Under continuing moderate drought, a protective iron-use effect, if present, should become more detectable in functional mesophyll.

text.zh: 铁依赖代谢与抗氧化网络的稳态维持可能增强，ROS峰值趋于受控。
text.en: Homeostatic support of iron-dependent metabolism and antioxidant networks may increase, with ROS peaks becoming constrained.

text.zh: 叶肉细胞存活、膜稳定性及叶绿体超微结构保持倾向改善。
text.en: Mesophyll cell viability, membrane stability, and chloroplast ultrastructure retention tend to improve.

text.zh: 叶片萎蔫延缓，单位叶面积的碳同化保持更好。
text.en: Leaf wilting may be delayed, with better maintenance of carbon assimilation per leaf area.

text.zh: 功能优势不必然提高总生长，因为气孔限制和碳分配仍主导干旱响应。
text.en: Functional benefit need not improve overall growth, because stomatal limitation and carbon allocation still dominate drought responses.

summary.zh: 保护效应可能达到可观察窗口，同时需警惕铁还原过强导致的氧化副作用。
summary.en: Protective effects may enter an observable window, while oxidative side effects from excessive iron reduction remain possible.

text.zh: 多数细胞可能维持较低氧化负荷，但铁富集微区可出现局部ROS升高。
text.en: Most cells may sustain a lower oxidative burden, but iron-enriched microdomains could show locally elevated ROS.

text.zh: 整体叶片电解质渗漏和失绿倾向可能低于对照，但空间异质性增加。
text.en: Overall leaf electrolyte leakage and chlorosis tendency may be lower than controls, but spatial heterogeneity may increase.

text.zh: 叶绿素荧光参数和叶片含水状态预计保持较好。
text.en: Chlorophyll-fluorescence parameters and leaf hydration are expected to be better maintained.

text.zh: 若FRO6定位不在叶绿体相关细胞区室，预测机制需调整。
text.en: If FRO6 is not localized to chloroplast-relevant cellular compartments, the proposed mechanism requires revision.

summary.zh: 中期持续胁迫下，FRO6上调可能主要表现为减缓损伤，而非完全解除干旱限制。
summary.en: During sustained mid-phase stress, FRO6 upregulation may chiefly slow damage rather than fully remove drought limitation.

text.zh: 细胞膜、蛋白质和光系统的氧化损伤累积可能较慢。
text.en: Accumulation of oxidative damage to membranes, proteins, and photosystems may be slower.

text.zh: 保绿和叶肉组织功能维持可能增强，坏死斑形成倾向下降。
text.en: Stay-green capacity and mesophyll function may improve, with less tendency for necrotic lesion formation.

text.zh: 叶面积扩张可能仍受抑，但现有叶片的光合保留预计较好。
text.en: Leaf-area expansion may remain inhibited, but photosynthetic retention in existing leaves is expected to be better.

text.zh: 该阶段无法仅凭FRO6推断根系供水或全株生物量改善。
text.en: At this stage, root water supply or whole-plant biomass improvement cannot be inferred from FRO6 alone.

summary.zh: 叶片可能进入适应与损伤并存阶段，保护程度取决于铁缓冲能力是否足够。
summary.en: Leaves may enter a phase where adaptation and injury coexist; protection depends on sufficient iron buffering.

text.zh: 抗氧化防御可能持续，但长期铁还原增强可提高局部Fenton反应负担。
text.en: Antioxidant defense may persist, but prolonged enhanced iron reduction can raise local Fenton-reaction burden.

text.zh: 大部分叶肉区域可能维持功能，易失水或衰老区域仍可出现失绿。
text.en: Most mesophyll regions may retain function, whereas water-prone or senescing regions can still become chlorotic.

text.zh: 相对对照，叶片损伤指数可能较低，但生长增益预计有限。
text.en: Relative to controls, leaf injury index may be lower, but growth gain is expected to remain limited.

text.zh: 长期结果高度依赖叶铁状态、抗氧化底物供应和转基因表达强度。
text.en: Longer-term outcomes depend strongly on leaf iron status, antioxidant substrate supply, and transgene expression strength.

summary.zh: 若干旱未加剧，FRO6相关保护可延续；若铁稳态失衡，则优势可能收窄。
summary.en: If drought does not intensify, FRO6-linked protection may persist; if iron homeostasis destabilizes, the advantage may narrow.

text.zh: 有序铁隔离时细胞稳态改善；隔离不足时游离铁和氧化损伤风险增加。
text.en: With orderly iron sequestration, cellular homeostasis improves; with insufficient sequestration, labile iron and oxidative-damage risk increase.

text.zh: 叶片功能保持可能优于对照，但老叶或叶尖的衰老差异可能缩小。
text.en: Leaf function may remain better than controls, but differences in senescence of older leaves or tips may narrow.

text.zh: 叶片存活和光合维持预计仍略优，但不代表全株抗旱性同步提升。
text.en: Leaf survival and photosynthetic maintenance are expected to remain modestly better, without implying parallel whole-plant drought tolerance.

text.zh: 叶片尺度推断向全株尺度转移的可靠性有限。
text.en: Reliability is limited when extrapolating from leaf-scale effects to whole-plant outcomes.

summary.zh: 持续干旱下，FRO6上调的净效应可能由保护性铁利用与氧化代价的平衡决定。
summary.en: Under prolonged drought, the net effect of FRO6 upregulation may be set by the balance of protective iron use and oxidative cost.

text.zh: 部分细胞维持代谢韧性，部分高铁或衰老细胞可能累积氧化压力。
text.en: Some cells may retain metabolic resilience, whereas high-iron or senescing cells may accumulate oxidative pressure.

text.zh: 叶片内保绿区与衰老区的差异可能更明显。
text.en: Contrasts between stay-green and senescing zones within the leaf may become more evident.

text.zh: 整体叶片功能预计仍有轻度优势，但优势趋于平台化。
text.en: Overall leaf function is expected to retain a slight advantage, but that advantage may plateau.

text.zh: 缺少FRO6过表达的剂量反应和铁毒性阈值信息。
text.en: Dose-response information for FRO6 overexpression and thresholds for iron toxicity are lacking.

summary.zh: 72小时内最稳妥的假设是叶片损伤被部分缓解，而非干旱胁迫被消除。
summary.en: By 72 h, the most defensible hypothesis is partial mitigation of leaf injury, not elimination of drought stress.

text.zh: 铁利用、抗氧化与ROS生成之间可能形成新的稳态，净效应因细胞区室而异。
text.en: A new balance among iron use, antioxidant activity, and ROS production may form, with net effects differing by cell compartment.

text.zh: 相对对照，叶肉完整性和保绿程度可能较高，但持续胁迫仍会驱动衰老。
text.en: Relative to controls, mesophyll integrity and greenness may be higher, although continued stress still drives senescence.

text.zh: 预期叶片光合保留、含水状态和可见损伤略有改善；全株生长不宜据此推定。
text.en: Leaf photosynthetic retention, hydration, and visible injury are expected to improve slightly; whole-plant growth should not be inferred.

text.zh: 结论适用于叶片假设；若脱离叶片或叶肉情境，FRO6推断的可转移性有限且应采用高不确定性。
text.en: This inference is leaf-specific; outside leaf or mesophyll contexts, FRO6 transferability is limited and uncertainty should be high.

mechanisms.zh: FRO6上调可假设性地提高叶片细胞铁(III)还原与可利用铁供应，从而支持铁依赖的叶绿体电子传递和抗氧化代谢。
mechanisms.en: FRO6 upregulation may hypothetically increase cellular ferric reduction and usable iron supply, supporting iron-dependent chloroplast electron transport and antioxidant metabolism.

mechanisms.zh: 中度干旱诱导的气孔限制和ROS压力会限制光合；更好的铁稳态可能减缓而非消除这些限制。
mechanisms.en: Moderate drought imposes stomatal limitation and ROS pressure on photosynthesis; improved iron homeostasis may attenuate rather than eliminate these limitations.

mechanisms.zh: 若还原产生的可交换铁未被安全螯合或隔离，Fenton化学可增加局部氧化损伤，因此中后期净效应可能呈混合方向。
mechanisms.en: If reduced exchangeable iron is not safely chelated or sequestered, Fenton chemistry can increase local oxidative damage; net mid-to-late effects may therefore be mixed.

assumptions.zh: FRO6上调发生于受处理叶片，并在叶肉或与叶绿体功能相关的细胞中具有功能。
assumptions.en: FRO6 upregulation occurs in treated leaves and is functional in mesophyll or chloroplast-relevant cells.

assumptions.zh: 干旱保持中度且持续，未发展为快速脱水、严重营养缺乏或不可逆组织死亡。
assumptions.en: Drought remains moderate and sustained, without progressing to rapid desiccation, severe nutrient deficiency, or irreversible tissue death.

assumptions.zh: 植株处于营养生长期、标准光周期和充足营养条件；未指定的基因型、表达剂量和叶位限制置信度。
assumptions.en: Plants are vegetative, under a standard photoperiod and adequate nutrition; unspecified genotype, expression dosage, and leaf position limit confidence.

assumptions.zh: 本轨迹主要适用于叶片/叶肉情境；在非叶片或非叶肉器官中，FRO6功能的可转移性有限，应采用高不确定性。
assumptions.en: This trajectory primarily applies to leaf/mesophyll contexts; in non-leaf or non-mesophyll organs, FRO6 functional transferability is limited and high uncertainty is warranted.

readouts.zh: 在6、12和18小时测定FRO6转录本/蛋白、铁(III)还原活性、可交换铁及铁螯合/储存相关表达。
readouts.en: At 6, 12, and 18 h, measure FRO6 transcript/protein, ferric-reduction activity, labile iron, and expression of iron-chelation/storage markers.

readouts.zh: 在18至48小时测定叶肉或叶绿体ROS、脂质过氧化、抗氧化酶活性、叶绿素含量及PSII荧光。
readouts.en: From 18 to 48 h, measure mesophyll or chloroplast ROS, lipid peroxidation, antioxidant enzyme activity, chlorophyll content, and PSII fluorescence.

readouts.zh: 在24至72小时测定气孔导度、净光合速率、叶水势或相对含水量、电解质渗漏、叶片卷曲与失绿。
readouts.en: From 24 to 72 h, measure stomatal conductance, net photosynthesis, leaf water potential or relative water content, electrolyte leakage, leaf rolling, and chlorosis.

readouts.zh: 用组织定位或细胞分选区分叶肉、表皮和维管区域，检验效应是否与FRO6表达域一致。
readouts.en: Use tissue localization or cell sorting to distinguish mesophyll, epidermal, and vascular regions and test whether effects match the FRO6 expression domain.

design.zh: 比较FRO6上调株与同遗传背景空载体/野生型，在充分灌水和匹配的中度干旱下进行时间序列；监测土壤水分并配对叶龄、叶位和取样时刻。加入表达剂量梯度或独立上调事件，并设置铁供应或铁螯合干预，以区分保护性铁利用与铁介导氧化伤害。
design.en: Compare FRO6-upregulated plants with matched-background empty-vector or wild-type controls under well-watered and matched moderate-drought conditions in a time series; monitor soil moisture and match leaf age, position, and sampling time. Include expression-dose gradients or independent upregulation events, plus iron-supply or iron-chelation perturbations, to distinguish protective iron use from iron-mediated oxidative injury.

title.zh: 水稻渐进中度干旱下的根—整株响应轨迹
title.en: Root-to-whole-plant trajectory of rice under progressive moderate drought

summary.zh: 根部首先感知水势下降，并启动快速节水与渗透调节信号。
summary.en: Roots first perceive lower water potential and initiate rapid water-conservation and osmotic-adjustment signaling.

text.zh: 细胞失水使膨压下降；Ca²⁺、ROS和ABA相关信号增强，并开始积累相容性溶质。
text.en: Cellular water loss lowers turgor; Ca²⁺, ROS, and ABA-related signaling rise while compatible-solute accumulation begins.

text.zh: 根系水力传导及伸长区细胞扩张开始受限。
text.en: Root hydraulic conductance and cell expansion in the elongation zone begin to be constrained.

text.zh: 整株蒸腾和叶片扩张出现轻度下降，尚无显著可见萎蔫。
text.en: Whole-plant transpiration and leaf expansion decline slightly, without pronounced visible wilting.

text.zh: 早期信号具有较强通用性，但实际幅度受基因型、根区含水量和蒸散需求影响。
text.en: Early signaling is broadly supported, but its magnitude depends on genotype, root-zone moisture, and evaporative demand.

summary.zh: ABA介导的根—冠协调增强，根细胞维持水势的代谢调整逐步展开。
summary.en: ABA-mediated root–shoot coordination strengthens, and metabolic adjustments to maintain cellular water status expand.

text.zh: ABA响应、抗氧化能力和糖类等渗透调节物合成增强；细胞分裂活性开始放缓。
text.en: ABA responses, antioxidant capacity, and production of osmolytes such as soluble sugars increase; cell-cycle activity begins to slow.

text.zh: 成熟根段可能提高水分再分配能力，而根尖伸长区的生长速率下降。
text.en: Mature root zones may enhance water redistribution, whereas growth slows in the root-tip elongation zone.

text.zh: 气孔导度和蒸腾进一步降低，地上部生长开始落后于对照。
text.en: Stomatal conductance and transpiration decline further, and shoot growth begins to lag behind the control.

text.zh: 假定土壤水分下降已足以诱导ABA信号，但未造成不可逆组织损伤。
text.en: This assumes soil drying is sufficient to induce ABA signaling but not irreversible tissue injury.

summary.zh: 转录与代谢重编程更加明显，资源由快速生长转向维持细胞稳态。
summary.en: Transcriptional and metabolic reprogramming becomes clearer, reallocating resources from rapid growth toward cellular homeostasis.

text.zh: 脱水响应基因表达、渗透保护物和ROS清除系统预计上调；蛋白与膜结构受保护。
text.en: Dehydration-response gene expression, osmoprotectants, and ROS-scavenging systems are expected to rise, protecting proteins and membranes.

text.zh: 根尖分生组织与伸长区的细胞产生和扩张受抑，侧根起始趋于保守。
text.en: Cell production and expansion in the root apical meristem and elongation zone are restrained, and lateral-root initiation becomes more conservative.

text.zh: 叶片生长及整株相对生长速率下降，植株保持较低耗水状态。
text.en: Leaf growth and whole-plant relative growth rate decline as the plant maintains lower water use.

text.zh: 根系发育反应高度依赖根龄与局部水分梯度，方向较稳健而程度不确定。
text.en: Root developmental responses depend strongly on root age and local moisture gradients; direction is more robust than magnitude.

summary.zh: 水分胁迫从快速信号阶段过渡到稳定的生长抑制与结构性适应阶段。
summary.en: Water stress shifts from rapid signaling to sustained growth restraint and structural adaptation.

text.zh: 渗透调节与抗氧化稳态维持增强，但细胞膨压、蛋白合成和扩张驱动力下降。
text.en: Osmotic adjustment and antioxidant homeostasis strengthen, but cellular turgor, protein synthesis, and expansion drive decline.

text.zh: 主根轴延伸受抑；较深或较湿根区的根段可能被优先维持。
text.en: Primary root-axis extension is restrained; root segments in deeper or wetter zones may be preferentially maintained.

text.zh: 冠层耗水、叶面积扩展和生物量积累均低于同龄对照。
text.en: Canopy water use, leaf-area expansion, and biomass accumulation are below those of age-matched controls.

text.zh: 是否形成明显深根优势取决于盆深、土壤剖面和原有根系架构。
text.en: Whether a clear deep-root advantage develops depends on pot depth, soil profile, and pre-existing root architecture.

summary.zh: 持续中度干旱强化保水策略，同时限制碳投入较高的新生根生长。
summary.en: Continued moderate drought reinforces water conservation while limiting carbon-intensive new root growth.

text.zh: 可溶性糖、氨基酸及保护性代谢物的周转增加，以支持渗透平衡和膜稳定性。
text.en: Turnover of soluble sugars, amino acids, and protective metabolites increases to support osmotic balance and membrane stability.

text.zh: 细根生成和根毛延长可能下降，根系总体吸水表面积扩张放慢。
text.en: Fine-root production and root-hair elongation may decline, slowing expansion of the total absorptive surface.

text.zh: 整株净碳同化和生长投入下降，水分利用策略转向保守。
text.en: Whole-plant net carbon gain and growth investment decline as water-use strategy becomes more conservative.

text.zh: 根毛反应可受磷供应和根际条件显著调节；此处假定养分供应充足。
text.en: Root-hair responses can be strongly modified by phosphorus supply and rhizosphere conditions; adequate nutrition is assumed here.

summary.zh: 适应性稳态逐渐建立，但持续低膨压使根系生长受限持续存在。
summary.en: An adaptive steady state gradually develops, but persistently low turgor continues to constrain root growth.

text.zh: ABA、渗透调节物和抗氧化系统维持在较高运行水平，未预期出现广泛细胞死亡。
text.en: ABA-related activity, osmolytes, and antioxidant systems remain elevated, with no widespread cell death expected.

text.zh: 根尖伸长和侧根外生速率持续偏低，根系周转趋于节约。
text.en: Root-tip elongation and lateral-root emergence remain lower, and root turnover becomes more resource-conservative.

text.zh: 植株高度、叶片展开及干物质累积的差距逐渐显现。
text.en: Differences in plant height, leaf unfolding, and dry-matter accumulation become increasingly apparent.

text.zh: 中度、渐进胁迫通常允许稳态适应；若实际干旱更强，损伤性过程会更早出现。
text.en: Moderate progressive stress generally permits steady-state adaptation; stronger actual drought would bring damaging processes earlier.

summary.zh: 根—冠水分平衡被重新设定，优先维持关键组织而非最大化生长。
summary.en: Root–shoot water balance is reset to prioritize maintenance of essential tissues rather than maximal growth.

text.zh: 维持代谢和修复需求升高，而细胞周期与生物合成活动保持受抑。
text.en: Maintenance metabolism and repair demand rise, while cell-cycle and biosynthetic activity remain suppressed.

text.zh: 存活根段维持功能性水分运输，但新根形成和组织增厚受到限制。
text.en: Surviving root segments maintain functional water transport, but new root formation and tissue thickening are constrained.

text.zh: 气孔关闭维持较强，光合受限使整株生长抑制加深。
text.en: Stomatal closure remains substantial, and photosynthetic limitation deepens whole-plant growth inhibition.

text.zh: 根水力变化可能因水通道蛋白调控而双向变化，故组织尺度采用混合方向。
text.en: Root hydraulics may shift in either direction through aquaporin regulation, so a mixed tissue-scale direction is used.

summary.zh: 两天持续胁迫后，生长抑制成为主要可测表型，保护性代谢仍在维持。
summary.en: After two days of sustained stress, growth inhibition becomes the dominant measurable phenotype while protective metabolism is maintained.

text.zh: 抗氧化酶活性、渗透保护和膜脂重塑预计保持增强，以限制氧化与脱水伤害。
text.en: Antioxidant enzyme activity, osmoprotection, and membrane-lipid remodeling are expected to remain elevated to limit oxidative and dehydration damage.

text.zh: 根系总长度和活跃根尖比例的增量低于对照，尤其在较干表层。
text.en: Increments in total root length and the proportion of active root tips are lower than in controls, especially in drier surface layers.

text.zh: 叶面积、生物量和蒸腾持续低于对照，但中度处理下植株总体仍应存活。
text.en: Leaf area, biomass, and transpiration remain below controls, although plants should remain viable under moderate stress.

text.zh: 该预测针对维持的中度胁迫，不外推至快速致死性失水。
text.en: This projection applies to maintained moderate stress and does not extend to rapidly lethal dehydration.

summary.zh: 碳分配与根系构型的可塑性开始影响后续表现，但短期内以生长节流为主。
summary.en: Plasticity in carbon allocation and root architecture begins to shape later performance, but short-term growth throttling remains dominant.

text.zh: 保护性碳代谢维持增强，但可用于新细胞构建的碳和能量供应减少。
text.en: Protective carbon metabolism remains enhanced, but carbon and energy available for constructing new cells decrease.

text.zh: 若存在较湿深层土壤，根系功能可向该区域偏移；整体根生物量增长仍偏低。
text.en: If wetter deep soil is available, root function may shift toward that zone; overall root biomass gain remains low.

text.zh: 根冠比可能相对升高或保持，但这是由地上部生长受抑和环境剖面共同决定的。
text.en: Root-to-shoot ratio may increase or remain unchanged, depending jointly on shoot-growth inhibition and the soil moisture profile.

text.zh: 根冠比及深层觅水需要土壤空间信息；当前情景未说明容器深度和水分剖面。
text.en: Root-to-shoot ratio and deep-water foraging require spatial soil information; container depth and moisture profile are unspecified.

summary.zh: 持续适应的代价表现为长期生长潜力下降，同时维持耐旱保护状态。
summary.en: The cost of sustained adaptation appears as reduced long-term growth potential while drought-protective status is maintained.

text.zh: 渗透与氧化还原稳态处于持续调整状态；若水势稳定，急性损伤信号不应继续快速累积。
text.en: Osmotic and redox homeostasis remain under sustained adjustment; if water potential stabilizes, acute damage signals should not continue to accumulate rapidly.

text.zh: 根伸长速率维持低位，较老根段对吸水功能的贡献相对增加。
text.en: Root elongation rate remains low, and older root segments contribute relatively more to water uptake function.

text.zh: 整株生长延迟持续，叶片可能呈现较保守的水分状态和较低的蒸腾。
text.en: Whole-plant growth delay persists, with leaves likely maintaining a more water-conservative state and lower transpiration.

text.zh: 预测假定处理强度维持稳定；水势继续下降会提高损伤与衰老风险。
text.en: The projection assumes stable treatment intensity; further water-potential decline would increase risks of injury and senescence.

summary.zh: 植株进入受控的低生长稳态，根系功能保持与新生长之间的权衡更加明确。
summary.en: The plant enters a controlled low-growth steady state, with a clearer trade-off between root-function maintenance and new growth.

text.zh: 维持存活和抗逆的转录程序持续，而分裂与扩张相关过程持续受限。
text.en: Transcriptional programs supporting survival and stress tolerance persist, while division- and expansion-related processes remain constrained.

text.zh: 根系新生组织供应偏低，吸水能力主要依赖既有功能根及局部水分可利用性。
text.en: Supply of newly formed root tissue is low, and water uptake depends mainly on existing functional roots and local water availability.

text.zh: 与同龄对照相比，株高、叶面积和累积生物量的差距进一步扩大。
text.en: Gaps in plant height, leaf area, and cumulative biomass relative to age-matched controls widen further.

text.zh: 水稻品种间的耐旱机制差异较大，因此不预设特定根系构型结果。
text.en: Drought-tolerance mechanisms vary substantially among rice genotypes, so no specific root architectural outcome is presumed.

summary.zh: 三天中度渐进干旱预计形成可逆但代价较高的耐受状态：保水优先，生长受限。
summary.en: After three days of progressive moderate drought, a reversible but costly tolerance state is expected: water conservation is prioritized over growth.

text.zh: 若胁迫未加剧，细胞保护、渗透调节和抗氧化能力维持高位，并限制不可逆损伤。
text.en: If stress does not intensify, cellular protection, osmotic adjustment, and antioxidant capacity remain elevated and limit irreversible injury.

text.zh: 根系长度、分枝和活跃生长区的累计增量低于对照，但主要根组织预计仍保持活性。
text.en: Cumulative gains in root length, branching, and active growth zones are below controls, but major root tissues are expected to remain viable.

text.zh: 整株表现为较低蒸腾、较慢叶片与根系生长及较少生物量；复水后可能部分恢复。
text.en: The whole plant shows lower transpiration, slower leaf and root growth, and less biomass; partial recovery may occur after rewatering.

text.zh: 可逆性取决于实际土壤水势、品种和复水速度；未指定这些条件限制了置信度。
text.en: Reversibility depends on actual soil water potential, cultivar, and rewatering rate; these unspecified conditions limit confidence.

mechanisms.zh: 根部水势下降可通过水力信号、Ca²⁺和ROS快速传递，并促进ABA相关根—冠协调。
mechanisms.en: Lower root water potential can be conveyed through hydraulic, Ca²⁺, and ROS signals and promote ABA-related root–shoot coordination.

mechanisms.zh: 渗透调节物、抗氧化系统与膜脂重塑有助于维持细胞膨压、氧化还原稳态和膜完整性。
mechanisms.en: Osmolytes, antioxidant systems, and membrane-lipid remodeling help maintain cellular turgor, redox balance, and membrane integrity.

mechanisms.zh: 低膨压、气孔关闭和碳同化受限会共同抑制细胞扩张、根系新生长及整株生物量积累。
mechanisms.en: Low turgor, stomatal closure, and constrained carbon assimilation together suppress cell expansion, new root growth, and whole-plant biomass accumulation.

mechanisms.zh: 根系对局部水分梯度的响应可改变功能根的空间贡献，但是否形成深根优势取决于土壤剖面。
mechanisms.en: Root responses to local moisture gradients can alter the spatial contribution of functional roots, but a deep-root advantage depends on the soil profile.

assumptions.zh: 处理为渐进、均一或已知水分梯度的中度干旱，并在72小时内保持；未发生淹水、盐害或严重高温。
assumptions.en: Stress is progressive moderate drought with uniform or characterized moisture gradients, maintained for 72 h; flooding, salinity, and severe heat are absent.

assumptions.zh: 材料为营养生长期、养分充足且初始健康的水稻；具体基因型、根龄和根区结构未指定。
assumptions.en: Plants are healthy, adequately nourished vegetative-stage rice; genotype, root age, and root-zone structure are unspecified.

assumptions.zh: 预测相对于每个时间点同龄、未处理对照，描述机制性假设而非实验结论。
assumptions.en: Predictions are relative to age-matched untreated controls at each timepoint and are mechanistic hypotheses rather than experimental conclusions.

assumptions.zh: 盆栽或田间土壤深度、质地及水分剖面未提供，因此深层觅水和根冠比分配的可迁移性有限。
assumptions.en: Pot or field soil depth, texture, and moisture profile are not provided; therefore, inference about deep-water foraging and root-to-shoot allocation has limited transferability.

readouts.zh: 土壤与根区水势、叶水势、相对含水量及根系水力传导。
readouts.en: Soil and root-zone water potential, leaf water potential, relative water content, and root hydraulic conductance.

readouts.zh: ABA、脯氨酸、可溶性糖、ROS/脂质过氧化指标及抗氧化酶活性。
readouts.en: ABA, proline, soluble sugars, ROS/lipid-peroxidation markers, and antioxidant-enzyme activity.

readouts.zh: 根尖细胞分裂与伸长、根长密度、侧根数、根毛性状及活根比例。
readouts.en: Root-tip cell division and elongation, root-length density, lateral-root number, root-hair traits, and live-root fraction.

readouts.zh: 气孔导度、蒸腾、光合速率、叶面积、根冠生物量和复水后的恢复程度。
readouts.en: Stomatal conductance, transpiration, photosynthetic rate, leaf area, root and shoot biomass, and recovery after rewatering.

readouts.zh: 在6、12、24、48和72小时进行信号、代谢与表型联合采样。
readouts.en: Perform integrated signaling, metabolic, and phenotypic sampling at 6, 12, 24, 48, and 72 h.

design.zh: 设置同龄充分浇水对照、渐进中度干旱处理及可选复水组；连续记录土壤含水量和蒸散，并在根尖、成熟根段及地上部分别采样。采用至少两个水稻基因型或将结论限定于单一基因型，以检验基因型依赖性。
design.en: Include age-matched well-watered controls, progressive moderate drought, and an optional rewatering group; continuously track soil moisture and transpiration, and sample root tips, mature root zones, and shoots separately. Use at least two rice genotypes, or explicitly limit inference to one genotype, to test genotype dependence.

title.zh: 水稻根系中度干旱后复水的恢复轨迹假说
title.en: Hypothesized recovery trajectory after rewatering moderately droughted rice roots

summary.zh: 复水首先解除水分亏缺；早期恢复以水力状态和快速信号重置为主。
summary.en: Rewatering first relieves water deficit; early recovery is dominated by hydraulic restoration and rapid signal resetting.

text.zh: 根细胞水势和膨压回升，膜运输与离子稳态开始恢复；仍可能保留短暂的ROS和Ca²⁺信号。
text.en: Root-cell water potential and turgor rise, while membrane transport and ion homeostasis begin to recover; transient ROS and Ca²⁺ signaling may persist.

text.zh: 根系吸水和木质部供水提高，但不同根轴和根龄的恢复可能不同步。
text.en: Root water uptake and xylem water supply increase, but recovery may be asynchronous among root axes and ages.

text.zh: 可见根伸长通常尚未明显改变，整株蒸腾恢复可能快于根生长。
text.en: Visible root elongation is usually not yet markedly changed; whole-plant transpiration may recover faster than root growth.

text.zh: 水力恢复的方向较可靠，但速度取决于土壤再润湿均一性和根系受损程度。
text.en: The direction of hydraulic recovery is well supported, but its speed depends on uniformity of soil rewetting and root injury.

summary.zh: 渗透调节和胁迫信号开始回调，根部进入代谢恢复窗口。
summary.en: Osmotic adjustment and stress signaling begin to reset, placing roots in a metabolic recovery window.

text.zh: ABA/脱水响应倾向下降，而能量代谢、蛋白修复和抗氧化清除仍可维持较高水平。
text.en: ABA/dehydration responses tend to decline, whereas energy metabolism, protein repair, and antioxidant detoxification may remain elevated.

text.zh: 皮层和维管组织的径向水分运输改善，根际重新湿润可增强矿质离子供给。
text.en: Radial water transport through cortex and vasculature improves, and rhizosphere rewetting can enhance mineral-ion supply.

text.zh: 根系生长表型多仍滞后；叶片展开和气孔恢复可开始改善整株碳同化。
text.en: Root-growth phenotypes mostly remain delayed; improving leaf expansion and stomatal recovery can begin to restore whole-plant carbon assimilation.

text.zh: 转录和代谢回调受原干旱持续时间及复水后养分可利用性影响。
text.en: Transcriptional and metabolic resetting depends on prior drought duration and nutrient availability after rewatering.

summary.zh: 细胞修复逐渐转向恢复性生长准备，但应激记忆可暂时保留。
summary.en: Cellular repair progressively shifts toward preparation for renewed growth, although stress memory can temporarily persist.

text.zh: 糖代谢、ATP供给和细胞壁前体合成增强；受胁迫损伤较重的细胞仍可能维持自噬或修复。
text.en: Sugar metabolism, ATP supply, and cell-wall precursor synthesis increase; heavily stressed cells may still maintain autophagy or repair.

text.zh: 根尖分生区可重新活化，但老根水力恢复与新侧根启动并不必然同步。
text.en: The root apical meristem may reactivate, but hydraulic recovery of older roots need not coincide with initiation of new lateral roots.

text.zh: 主根或存活根轴的伸长有望开始恢复，但总体根系长度变化仍较小。
text.en: Elongation of primary or surviving root axes may begin to resume, though total root-system length may still change little.

text.zh: 是否出现快速生长恢复取决于根尖在干旱期间是否保持活性。
text.en: Whether growth resumes rapidly depends on whether root tips retained activity during drought.

summary.zh: 复水后约一天，根系由急性应答转入结构与资源分配的恢复阶段。
summary.en: At about one day after rewatering, roots transition from acute responses to structural and resource-allocation recovery.

text.zh: 多数脱水保护程序继续减弱，但细胞分裂、伸长和细胞壁重塑相关活动上升。
text.en: Most dehydration-protection programs continue to decline, while activities linked to cell division, elongation, and cell-wall remodeling rise.

text.zh: 根尖伸长区和维管组织的功能协调改善，侧根原基可进入启动或继续发育。
text.en: Functional coordination of the root elongation zone and vasculature improves, and lateral-root primordia may initiate or resume development.

text.zh: 根伸长速率预计回升，但未必达到同龄未受旱植株水平。
text.en: Root elongation rate is expected to rise, but may not reach that of age-matched untreated plants.

text.zh: 恢复不是干旱响应的简单逆转；持续的发育重编程可能限制完全恢复。
text.en: Recovery is not a simple reversal of drought responses; persistent developmental reprogramming may limit full restoration.

summary.zh: 水分供应较稳定时，根系资源分配开始支持新生组织和吸收能力重建。
summary.en: If water supply remains stable, root resource allocation begins to support new tissues and rebuilding of uptake capacity.

text.zh: 细胞伸长、膜脂更新和养分转运能力增强，氧化还原状态趋向稳态。
text.en: Cell elongation, membrane-lipid renewal, and nutrient-transport capacity increase, while redox status moves toward steady state.

text.zh: 细根和侧根形成潜力上升，根表吸收界面的有效面积开始扩大。
text.en: Potential for fine-root and lateral-root formation rises, and the effective absorptive surface begins to expand.

text.zh: 根系活力和吸水能力预期提高，地上部恢复对根系供水的依赖增强。
text.en: Root activity and water-uptake capacity are expected to improve, increasing support for shoot recovery.

text.zh: 侧根响应对品种、根区氧气和复水后的土壤机械阻力较敏感。
text.en: Lateral-root responses are sensitive to genotype, root-zone oxygen, and soil mechanical resistance after rewatering.

summary.zh: 恢复性生长与残余防御并存，根系功能趋于重新整合。
summary.en: Restorative growth coexists with residual defense, and root functions move toward reintegration.

text.zh: 增殖和伸长相关过程增强，同时部分抗氧化、渗透调节和损伤修复程序仍高于未受旱对照。
text.en: Proliferation- and elongation-related processes strengthen, while some antioxidant, osmotic, and damage-repair programs remain above untreated controls.

text.zh: 根尖活动和根毛功能可增强，径向吸收与轴向运输的耦合进一步改善。
text.en: Root-tip activity and root-hair function may increase, further improving coupling of radial uptake and axial transport.

text.zh: 总体根系生长恢复更清晰，但根冠大小仍可能落后于未受旱植株。
text.en: Overall root growth recovery becomes clearer, although root-system size may still lag behind untreated plants.

text.zh: 残余胁迫程序的持续时间受干旱强度和复水后环境稳定性影响。
text.en: The persistence of residual stress programs depends on drought severity and environmental stability after rewatering.

summary.zh: 根系进入功能补偿阶段，新增吸收组织可能逐步弥补干旱期的生长损失。
summary.en: Roots enter a functional-compensation phase, in which new absorptive tissues may progressively offset drought-period growth losses.

text.zh: 蛋白合成、细胞壁沉积和养分同化保持活跃，细胞水分状态接近恢复稳态。
text.en: Protein synthesis, cell-wall deposition, and nutrient assimilation remain active, while cellular water status approaches a recovered steady state.

text.zh: 侧根伸出和根毛发育可增加局部吸收能力；老化或受损根段未必完全恢复。
text.en: Lateral-root emergence and root-hair development can increase local uptake capacity; aged or injured root segments may not fully recover.

text.zh: 根系活力、养分吸收和整株水分平衡继续改善。
text.en: Root vigor, nutrient uptake, and whole-plant water balance continue to improve.

text.zh: 补偿性建根的幅度尤其依赖基因型及复水后氮、磷供应。
text.en: The magnitude of compensatory root formation particularly depends on genotype and post-rewatering nitrogen and phosphorus supply.

summary.zh: 两天后，急性水分胁迫特征应大多消退，但根系结构遗留效应仍可存在。
summary.en: By two days, acute water-stress features should mostly subside, but structural legacy effects can remain in roots.

text.zh: 细胞水分和基础代谢接近稳态；少数修复、抗氧化或表观调控状态可能仍偏离对照。
text.en: Cellular water status and basal metabolism approach steady state; some repair, antioxidant, or epigenetic states may still differ from controls.

text.zh: 存活根段的导水和吸收功能接近恢复，而根密度与根序结构仍反映先前干旱。
text.en: Hydraulic and uptake function of surviving root segments approaches recovery, whereas root density and branching architecture still reflect prior drought.

text.zh: 根系增长和地上部恢复持续，但累计生物量缺口通常仍存在。
text.en: Root growth and shoot recovery continue, but a cumulative biomass deficit usually remains.

text.zh: 中度干旱定义较宽，若根系发生不可逆损伤，功能恢复会低于此预测。
text.en: Moderate drought is broadly defined; irreversible root injury would reduce functional recovery relative to this prediction.

summary.zh: 根系由恢复转向适应后的稳态建立，生长与资源获取重新协调。
summary.en: Roots shift from recovery toward establishment of a post-stress steady state, re-coordinating growth and resource acquisition.

text.zh: 多数细胞过程趋于未受旱水平附近，但发育和碳分配信号可保留应激历史。
text.en: Most cellular processes approach untreated levels, but developmental and carbon-allocation signals may retain stress history.

text.zh: 新形成的侧根和根毛继续提高吸收面积，根系内功能异质性逐步降低。
text.en: New lateral roots and root hairs continue to enlarge absorptive area, while functional heterogeneity within roots gradually declines.

text.zh: 整株水分利用和营养供给趋稳，根系仍可能表现温和的补偿性生长。
text.en: Whole-plant water use and nutrient supply stabilize, while roots may still show modest compensatory growth.

text.zh: 补偿生长并非必然；若同化物优先用于叶片恢复，根系增量可能受限。
text.en: Compensatory growth is not inevitable; if assimilates are prioritized for leaf recovery, root gains may be limited.

summary.zh: 在持续适宜供水下，根系功能预计接近新的后胁迫平衡，而非完全回到原始轨迹。
summary.en: Under continued favorable watering, root function is expected to approach a new post-stress equilibrium rather than fully return to its original trajectory.

text.zh: 水分、离子和氧化还原稳态基本恢复；持续的转录或染色质记忆仍可能影响后续响应。
text.en: Water, ion, and redox homeostasis are largely restored; persistent transcriptional or chromatin memory may still influence later responses.

text.zh: 根部导水和养分运输趋于稳定，新根贡献逐步替代部分较弱老根功能。
text.en: Root hydraulics and nutrient transport stabilize, with new roots progressively replacing part of the function of weaker older roots.

text.zh: 根系生长可维持恢复态，但与未受旱植株相比的大小差距未必消失。
text.en: Root growth can maintain a recovered state, but the size gap relative to untreated plants may not disappear.

text.zh: 长期平衡受盆土体积、根区缺氧风险和后续蒸散需求影响。
text.en: The longer-term equilibrium is affected by pot volume, risk of root-zone hypoxia, and subsequent transpiration demand.

summary.zh: 恢复后的根系结构与功能继续整合，先前干旱造成的时序差异逐渐显现。
summary.en: Recovered root structure and function continue to integrate, and timing differences caused by prior drought become more evident.

text.zh: 基础细胞活动稳定，局部新根细胞仍保持较高分裂和伸长活性。
text.en: Basal cellular activity is stable, while cells in localized new roots retain high division and elongation activity.

text.zh: 新生吸收组织增加，但旧根段的寿命、通透性或木质化状态可能与对照不同。
text.en: New absorptive tissues increase, but the lifespan, permeability, or lignification of older root segments may differ from controls.

text.zh: 根系功能对整株恢复的支持增强，累计生长损失则可能仅缓慢缩小。
text.en: Root function provides stronger support for whole-plant recovery, whereas cumulative growth loss may narrow only slowly.

text.zh: 结构遗留效应的方向和大小对水稻品种及根序组成敏感。
text.en: The direction and magnitude of structural legacy effects are sensitive to rice genotype and root-order composition.

summary.zh: 三天后，根系应达到稳定的后复水状态；功能恢复可明显而形态完全补偿未必发生。
summary.en: By three days, roots should reach a stable post-rewatering state; functional recovery may be substantial without complete morphological compensation.

text.zh: 细胞水分和代谢稳态总体恢复，但胁迫记忆可能改变其对下一次干旱的响应阈值。
text.en: Cellular water and metabolic homeostasis are broadly restored, but stress memory may alter response thresholds to a later drought.

text.zh: 根系吸收和运输网络较稳定，而根系构型、根龄分布及受损根段仍可区别于对照。
text.en: Root uptake and transport networks are relatively stable, whereas root architecture, age distribution, and damaged segments may still differ from controls.

text.zh: 整株恢复持续，根系生物量和构型可能仍低于或不同于同龄未受旱植株。
text.en: Whole-plant recovery continues, but root biomass and architecture may remain below or differ from age-matched untreated plants.

text.zh: 该终点假设复水持续且无积水、病害或养分限制等次生胁迫。
text.en: This endpoint assumes continued rewatering without secondary stress from waterlogging, disease, or nutrient limitation.

mechanisms.zh: 复水提高土壤—根—木质部水势梯度，先恢复细胞膨压和根系水力功能。
mechanisms.en: Rewatering restores the soil-root-xylem water-potential gradient, first improving cell turgor and root hydraulic function.

mechanisms.zh: ABA、Ca²⁺和ROS等早期胁迫信号逐步重置，但损伤修复与氧化还原调节可持续更久。
mechanisms.en: Early stress signals such as ABA, Ca²⁺, and ROS progressively reset, whereas damage repair and redox regulation can persist longer.

mechanisms.zh: 能量代谢、膜与细胞壁修复支持根尖再活化、根毛功能恢复及侧根形成。
mechanisms.en: Energy metabolism and repair of membranes and cell walls support root-tip reactivation, root-hair recovery, and lateral-root formation.
